title: Version timeline with draft snapshots and restore
Ship a Revision Queue board that turns scene issues into ordered fix tasks
Add manuscript import wizard for .docx/.txt with chapter-scene detection
description:
## Problem Many target users already draft in Google Docs, Word, or plain text. Without import, switching costs are high and onboarding stalls before users experience the app's structure/planning advantages. ## Target user Writers migrating an in-progress manuscript from general-purpose editors into a story-focused workspace. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase activation rate from signup to first meaningful edit session - Reduce drop-off during first-time story creation - Increase imported manuscripts that reach at least one saved revision inside app ## Scope - Add an import entry point from Home/Stories (Import manuscript) - Support .docx and .txt uploads up to a defined size limit - Parse headings into chapter candidates with editable mapping before final import - Create scenes from detected chapter sections with fallback to single-scene import - Show import summary: chapters/scenes created, unresolved formatting warnings ## Non-goals - Perfect formatting parity for every Word style - PDF import - One-click cloud provider sync integrations ## Acceptance criteria - User can upload .docx or .txt and complete import without leaving app - User can review/edit chapter mapping before finalizing import - Imported manuscript opens in chapter/scene navigator with preserved text content - Unsupported formatting is surfaced as non-blocking warnings, not silent data loss - Failed imports provide clear, actionable error message and retry path ## Priority rationale Import removes a major adoption barrier and accelerates progress toward the company goal by letting real writers bring real work into the app immediately. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Add a 3-step manuscript import wizard (BIN-68) that lets writers upload a `.txt` or `.docx` file, review auto-detected chapters and scenes, and create a fully-populated Story with Scenes in localStorage — all within a full-screen modal overlay — without any backend. --- ## Scope and files ### New files (10) | Path | Purpose | |------|---------| | `src/types/importWizard.ts` | `ParsedManuscript`, `ChapterBlock`, `SceneBlock`, `MappingRow` types | | `src/lib/importParser.ts` | `parseTextFile()`, `parseMammothHtml()`, `deriveTitle()` | | `src/lib/importStory.ts` | `importManuscript()` — orchestrates Story + Scene + content creation | | `src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.tsx` | Wizard shell: step router, state machine, portal | | `src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.module.css` | Full-screen overlay, step layout, drag-drop zone, table styles | | `src/components/ImportWizard/StepUpload.tsx` | Step 1 UI: drag-drop area, file picker, size warning | | `src/components/ImportWizard/StepReview.tsx` | Step 2 UI: mapping table with inline edit, chapter dropdown, merge, up/down reorder | | `src/components/ImportWizard/StepSummary.tsx` | Step 3 UI: success count + "Open story" CTA | | `src/hooks/useImportWizard.ts` | State management hook for wizard (step, file, parsed data, mapped rows, loading, error) | | `src/components/ImportWizard/index.ts` | Barrel export | ### Modified files (4) | Path | Change | |------|--------| | `src/app/page.tsx` | Add `isWizardOpen` state; render ``; add "Import manuscript" outlined button in `.welcome` section | | `src/app/page.module.css` | Add `.actionRow`, `.importButton` styles | | `src/app/stories/page.tsx` | Add `isWizardOpen` state; render ``; add "Import" button in `.toolbar` | | `src/app/stories/page.module.css` | Add `.importButton` style | ### Install ``` npm install mammoth ``` `mammoth` ships its own types; `@types/mammoth` is not needed. --- ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 0 — Install mammoth ``` npm install mammoth ``` Verify `package.json` now lists `"mammoth"` under `dependencies`. No additional type package is required. --- ### Step 1 — Define shared types in `src/types/importWizard.ts` ```ts export interface SceneBlock { title: string; body: string; // full scene text summary: string; // first 150 chars of body, stripped of leading title line } export interface ChapterBlock { title: string; scenes: SceneBlock[]; } export interface ParsedManuscript { chapters: ChapterBlock[]; } // Working row used in Step 2 mapping table export interface MappingRow { id: string; // crypto.randomUUID() — UI key only chapterTitle: string; // user-editable chapter label sceneTitle: string; // user-editable scene title preview: string; // first 80 chars of body (read-only display) body: string; // full text, preserved for content save status: 'planned' | 'drafting' | 'done'; } ``` --- ### Step 2 — Build `src/lib/importParser.ts` Export the following functions: ```ts // Entry point for .txt files and the plain-text path of .docx export function parseTextFile(text: string): ParsedManuscript // Entry point when mammoth produces HTML (h1/h2 headings available) export function parseMammothHtml(html: string): ParsedManuscript // Derives a story title from a filename: strips extension, replaces hyphens/ // underscores with spaces, title-cases the result export function deriveTitle(filename: string): string ``` #### `parseTextFile` algorithm 1. Normalize line endings: replace `\r\n` and `\r` with `\n`. 2. Split into lines. 3. Iterate lines, tracking `currentChapter` (string) and `currentSceneLines` (string[]). Chapter detection (any single line matching one of): - `/^(chapter|ch\.?|act|part)\s+(\d+|[ivxlcdm]+|one|two|three|four|five|six|seven|eight|nine|ten)\b/i` - Line is ALL_CAPS, length >= 3, no sentence punctuation (not a decoration line) Scene break detection (any line matching): - Empty line followed by empty line (double-blank) — accumulate `currentSceneLines` into a scene when the blank pair appears - A line matching `/^\s*(\*\s*){3,}$/` (asterisk separators) - A line matching `/^\s*-{3,}\s*$/` (horizontal rule) - A line matching `/^#{1,3}\s/` (markdown headings — treat as scene break) 4. When a chapter header is detected: flush `currentSceneLines` as a scene into the current chapter if non-empty; push current chapter to result; start a new chapter. 5. When a scene break is detected: flush `currentSceneLines` as a scene. 6. At end of input: flush any remaining `currentSceneLines`. 7. Title extraction: first non-blank line of `currentSceneLines` (stripped). If blank, use `"Untitled Scene"`. 8. Summary extraction: join non-title scene lines, strip to 150 chars, trimmed. 9. Fallback: if zero chapters detected, wrap everything in one chapter titled `"Chapter 1"` with scenes. If zero scenes total, return one chapter with one scene containing all text. #### `parseMammothHtml` algorithm 1. Parse `

` tags as chapter titles, `

` tags as scene titles. 2. Collect text between headings as scene body `

` content. 3. If no `

` tags found, fall back to calling `parseTextFile` on the extracted plain text. 4. Use a lightweight DOM parser (create a temporary `document.createElement('div')` and set `.innerHTML`) — runs client-side only. 5. Summary: first 150 chars of `

` content per scene. --- ### Step 3 — Build `src/lib/importStory.ts` ```ts export async function importManuscript( title: string, rows: MappingRow[] ): Promise // returns new storyId ``` Algorithm: 1. `const story = createStory(title)` — uses existing `src/lib/stories.ts`. 2. Assemble full content string by joining `rows.map(r => r.body)` with `\n\n`. 3. `await saveContent(story.id, fullContent)` — uses existing `src/lib/autosave.ts`. 4. For each row in `rows` (in order): ``` addScene(story.id, { title: row.sceneTitle, chapter: row.chapterTitle, summary: row.body.slice(0, 150), status: row.status, intent: '', pov: '', }) ``` This clears the mock-data initialization because `getScenes` checks for existing localStorage data before falling back to mocks; a fresh story will have no stored scenes, but `addScene` writes to `story-{id}-scenes` directly. **Important**: call `addScene` in a loop after the story exists. The first call will hit the `getScenes` fallback that seeds mock data, then push the new scene on top. To avoid this, write a bulk helper or clear the mock seed: initialize the scenes key to `[]` before the loop by calling `localStorage.setItem(`story-${story.id}-scenes`, '[]')` at the start, then loop `addScene`. Alternatively, add a `bulkAddScenes` helper to `src/lib/scenes.ts` that sets all scenes at once — this is the cleaner approach. Plan includes adding this helper. 5. Return `story.id`. **Addendum — add `bulkAddScenes` to `src/lib/scenes.ts`** ```ts export function bulkAddScenes( storyId: string, scenes: Omit[] ): Scene[] ``` Writes the full array to `story-{storyId}-scenes` in one `localStorage.setItem` call, assigning sequential IDs and order values. This avoids the mock-data initialization problem and is O(1) writes regardless of scene count. --- ### Step 4 — Build `src/hooks/useImportWizard.ts` ```ts export type WizardStep = 1 | 2 | 3; export interface WizardState { step: WizardStep; file: File | null; storyTitle: string; rows: MappingRow[]; isLoading: boolean; parseError: string | null; sizeWarning: boolean; // true if file > 5MB createdStoryId: string | null; } export interface WizardActions { handleFileSelect: (file: File) => Promise; handleNext: () => Promise; // Step 1→2: triggers parse handleBack: () => void; // Step 2→1 handleTitleChange: (title: string) => void; handleRowUpdate: (id: string, updates: Partial) => void; handleMergeWithNext: (id: string) => void; handleMoveUp: (id: string) => void; handleMoveDown: (id: string) => void; handleImport: () => Promise; // Step 2→3: calls importManuscript handleReset: () => void; } ``` File parsing in `handleNext`: 1. Read file as `ArrayBuffer`. 2. If `.docx` extension: `import mammoth from 'mammoth'` (dynamic import to avoid SSR), call `mammoth.convertToHtml({ arrayBuffer })`, then `parseMammothHtml(result.value)`. 3. If `.txt` or any other extension: read as text with `FileReader`, call `parseTextFile(text)`. 4. Flatten `ParsedManuscript` into `MappingRow[]`: for each `ChapterBlock`, for each `SceneBlock`, push `{ id: crypto.randomUUID(), chapterTitle, sceneTitle, preview: body.slice(0,80), body, status: 'planned' }`. 5. Set `storyTitle` via `deriveTitle(file.name)`. 6. Advance to step 2. Error path: catch parse errors, set `parseError`, stay on step 1 but show error message. --- ### Step 5 — Build `src/components/ImportWizard/ImportWizard.tsx` (wizard shell) ```ts interface ImportWizardProps { isOpen: boolean; onClose: () => void; onImportComplete: (storyId: string) => void; } ``` Implementation notes: - Guard with `if (!isOpen) return null` (same pattern as `ConfirmDialog`). - Render via `ReactDOM.createPortal(content, document.body)` — prevents stacking context issues with any parent `z-index`. Import `createPortal` from `'react-dom'`. - Outer div: `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby="import-wizard-title"`. - `useEffect` to add/remove `keydown` handler for Escape key (calls `onClose` from step 1 or 2; from step 3 it navigates instead). - `useEffect` to lock body scroll: `document.body.style.overflow = 'hidden'` on mount, restore on unmount. - Focus management: `useRef` on the dialog container, call `.focus()` when `isOpen` becomes true. - Renders a step progress indicator (3 dots or numbered steps) and the current step component based on `state.step`. - Step 3 passes `createdStoryId` to `onImportComplete(storyId)` when the "Open story" button is clicked. --- ### Step 6 — Build `src/components/ImportWizard/StepUpload.tsx` UI elements: - Drop zone: `

` with `onDragOver`, `onDrop`, `onDragEnter`, `onDragLeave` handlers; toggles `.dropZoneActive` CSS class on drag enter/leave. - Hidden `` triggered by a "Browse files" button or clicking the drop zone. - Size warning: if `sizeWarning` prop is true, show inline warning banner: "This file is large (>5MB). Import may be slow." with a continue option — does NOT block proceeding. - Parse error display: if `parseError` is set, show "We couldn't detect chapters automatically. The whole file will be imported as one scene." — auto-applies fallback rather than blocking. - Empty file detection happens in the parser; `StepUpload` displays whatever error string is in `parseError`. - "Next" button disabled until a file is selected; shows spinner while `isLoading` is true. - "Cancel" button calls `onClose`. Accepted file types: `.txt` and `.docx` only. Validate on drop and file input `change` event; reject other types with an inline error message "Only .txt and .docx files are supported." --- ### Step 7 — Build `src/components/ImportWizard/StepReview.tsx` UI elements: - Editable story title field at the top (bound to `storyTitle`, calls `handleTitleChange`). - Table with columns: Chapter | Scene Title | Preview | Status | Actions. - Chapter column: `` on click, commits on blur/Enter, reverts on Escape. - Preview column: truncated read-only text (80 chars). - Status column: `` dropdown per row | | Merge with next | `handleMergeWithNext` in `useImportWizard` | | Scene reorder | `handleMoveUp`/`handleMoveDown` in `useImportWizard`; up/down arrow buttons in `StepReview` | | Step 3 success summary | `StepSummary.tsx` with N chapters, M scenes count | | "Open story" CTA navigates to story | `onImportComplete(storyId)` → `router.push` in calling page | | New Story created with filename-derived title | `deriveTitle(file.name)` → `createStory(title)` | | Scenes persisted to localStorage | `bulkAddScenes` in `importStory.ts` | | Full text saved via `saveContent` | `saveContent(story.id, fullText)` in `importManuscript` | | Entry point on Home page | "Import manuscript" button in `src/app/page.tsx` | | Entry point on Stories page | "Import" button in `src/app/stories/page.tsx` | | Parse failure graceful fallback | `parseError` state → shown in `StepUpload`; parser falls back to 1 chapter / 1 scene | | Empty file error | Parser detects empty text; sets `parseError = 'File appears empty'` | | Escape closes modal | `keydown` handler in `ImportWizard` | | Modal uses `--z-modal` | `.overlay` CSS uses `var(--z-modal)` | | Grayscale tokens only | All CSS uses only approved tokens from `tokens.css` | --- ## Handoff Assign to: Code Monkey

title: Character bible linked to manuscript mentions
description:
Inspired by Campfire and World Anvil reference systems. Problem: character details drift over long drafts, causing continuity errors. Outcome: writers maintain consistent character traits and relationships while drafting. Acceptance criteria: - Character profiles support core fields (bio, goals, relationships, appearance, voice notes). - Manuscript mentions auto-link to matching character entries with manual override. - Continuity warnings flag conflicting details (e.g., eye color mismatch) before export. - Character search and quick panel are available during editing. Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Add a Character Bible feature to the story writing app. Characters are the primary data object: each has a name, role, aliases, description, traits, and notes. The bible is accessible as a new `'characters'` view mode (full-page split-panel) and as a slide-out `CharacterQuickPanel` from the editor. Mention detection scans the manuscript for character names and aliases — no external NLP library — and surfaces per-chapter occurrence counts inside the character detail panel. A lightweight `ContinuityWarningBanner` flags POV inconsistencies and name variant overuse. --- ## Scope and files ### New files (15) | File | Purpose | |---|---| | `src/types/character.ts` | `Character` interface and related types | | `src/lib/characters.ts` | CRUD functions + `mentionScan` pure function | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterBible.tsx` | Full-page container: list + detail split | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterBible.module.css` | Layout, list, avatar | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterCard.tsx` | Single character row in list | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterCard.module.css` | Card + role badge styles | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterDetail.tsx` | Right-panel, inline editable fields + mention breakdown | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterDetail.module.css` | Detail panel styles | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterSearch.tsx` | Search input with filtered results | | `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterSearch.module.css` | Search input styles | | `src/components/CharacterBible/ContinuityWarningBanner.tsx` | Collapsible flag roll-up | | `src/components/CharacterBible/ContinuityWarningBanner.module.css` | Banner styles | | `src/components/CharacterQuickPanel/CharacterQuickPanel.tsx` | Slide-out drawer from editor | | `src/components/CharacterQuickPanel/CharacterQuickPanel.module.css` | Fixed right-drawer styles | ### Modified files (3) | File | Change | |---|---| | `src/types/character.ts` | New file — listed above | | `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx` | Add `'characters'` (and coordinate `'revision'` slot) to `ViewMode` union and render button | | `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` | Add `characters` branch to view-mode conditional; add `isCharPanelOpen` state; add Characters panel toggle button to editor header | | `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` | Add `.characterBibleLayout` and `.charPanelToggle` rules | --- ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 1 — Define the Character type (`src/types/character.ts`) Create the type file. Export: ```ts export type CharacterRole = 'protagonist' | 'antagonist' | 'supporting' | 'minor'; export interface Character { id: string; storyId: string; name: string; role: CharacterRole; aliases: string[]; // variations used for mention detection description: string; traits: string[]; // stored as array; UI renders comma-separated notes: string; createdAt: number; updatedAt: number; } export interface MentionEntry { total: number; byChapter: Record; // chapter label -> count } export type MentionMap = Record; // character id -> MentionEntry ``` No runtime logic here — pure type declarations. --- ### Step 2 — Build `src/lib/characters.ts` Model this file exactly after `src/lib/scenes.ts`. Key decisions: - Storage key: `story-{storyId}-characters` (matches scenes key pattern `story-${storyId}-scenes`) - Content key read via the existing exported `loadContent` from `src/lib/autosave.ts` — `story_${storyId}_content` - All functions guard with `typeof window === 'undefined'` #### 2a — CRUD functions ```ts const storageKey = (storyId: string) => `story-${storyId}-characters`; export function getCharacters(storyId: string): Character[] // reads localStorage, parses, returns sorted by createdAt asc (stable display order) // returns [] if nothing stored yet (no mock data needed) export function saveCharacters(storyId: string, characters: Character[]): void // private helper: writes JSON to localStorage, guards SSR export function addCharacter( storyId: string, data: Omit ): Character // crypto.randomUUID() for id, Date.now() for both timestamps // appends, re-saves export function updateCharacter( storyId: string, characterId: string, updates: Partial> ): Character | null // merges updates + sets updatedAt = Date.now() // returns null if not found export function deleteCharacter(storyId: string, characterId: string): void // filters out, re-saves ``` #### 2b — `mentionScan` pure function ```ts export function mentionScan( storyId: string, characters: Character[], scenes: Scene[] // passed in; used only for chapter label derivation ): MentionMap ``` Logic: 1. Call `loadContent(storyId)` from `src/lib/autosave.ts` — returns the raw manuscript string or `null`. 2. If `null` or empty string, return an empty `MentionMap` `{}`. 3. For the chapter breakdown, build a `Record` by scanning the content for chapter headings. Since the manuscript is free-form text, use the scenes array to identify chapter labels. Because the manuscript has no structural markers, the v1 strategy is: for each unique chapter label from `scenes`, compute a notional "chapter start offset" by splitting content into equal slices equal to the number of distinct chapters. This avoids any NLP — it is an approximation but sufficient for v1 continuity hints. Concrete algorithm for chapter attribution: - `const chapters = [...new Set(scenes.map(s => s.chapter))].sort()` — deduplicated, sorted array of chapter labels. - `const chunkSize = Math.ceil(content.length / chapters.length)` — divide content into equal segments. - For each character match, determine which segment the match falls in: `const chapterIndex = Math.floor(match.index / chunkSize)`. - Attribute to `chapters[chapterIndex]`. - If no scenes exist, attribute all matches to a single bucket `'Manuscript'`. 4. For each `character` in `characters`: - Build list of terms: `[character.name, ...character.aliases].filter(Boolean)` - For each term: `new RegExp('\\b' + escapeRegex(term) + '\\b', 'gi')` - Accumulate all matches across all terms (dedup by position to avoid double-counting name + alias that are the same string) - Build `MentionEntry { total, byChapter }` 5. Private helper `escapeRegex(s: string): string` — escapes special regex characters so names like "O'Brien" do not throw. ```ts function escapeRegex(s: string): string { return s.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&'); } ``` Return the `MentionMap`. This function has no side effects; it is safe to call on any render. #### 2c — `computeContinuityWarnings` ```ts export interface ContinuityWarning { type: 'pov_mismatch' | 'name_variant'; characterId: string; characterName: string; message: string; } export function computeContinuityWarnings( characters: Character[], scenes: Scene[], mentions: MentionMap, povThreshold = 3 ): ContinuityWarning[] ``` Logic: - **POV mismatch**: for each scene with a `pov` field set, if `mentions[char.id]?.byChapter[scene.chapter] >= povThreshold` but `scene.pov !== char.name` and `char.role === 'protagonist'` — push a warning. This is a best-effort signal, not a hard error. - **Name variant**: if `char.aliases.length > 0`, check if any alias independently has mentions > 0 across chapters. If the alias is used in the text alongside the primary name (both have hits in the same chapter), surface "Both '{name}' and '{alias}' used in {chapter}". Return array of `ContinuityWarning[]`. Empty array = no warnings. --- ### Step 3 — `CharacterSearch` component `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterSearch.tsx` Props: ```ts interface CharacterSearchProps { value: string; onChange: (query: string) => void; } ``` Renders a single `` with: - `aria-label="Search characters"` - `placeholder="Search by name or alias…"` - Controlled by parent via `value` / `onChange` - No internal debounce — parent holds query state; filtering is cheap for small lists CSS: full-width input, `--color-fog-200` background, `--border-width` solid `--color-fashion-300` border, `--border-radius-sm`, clears on `Escape` (calls `onChange('')`). --- ### Step 4 — `CharacterCard` component `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterCard.tsx` Props: ```ts interface CharacterCardProps { character: Character; mentionCount: number; // total from MentionMap or 0 isSelected: boolean; onClick: () => void; } ``` Renders a `
  • ` with: - Initials avatar: first letter of first word + first letter of last word (if name has a space), else first two letters. `48px` circle, `--color-polar-100` bg, `--color-carbon-600` text, `font-size: var(--font-size-lg)`. - Character name (`--font-size-base`, `--font-weight-semibold`, `--color-black-700`) - Role badge — inline `` styled by role per design spec: - `protagonist`: bg `--color-black-700`, text `--color-white`, `--border-radius-sm`, padding `2px 6px` - `antagonist`: bg transparent, border `1px solid --color-carbon-600`, text `--color-carbon-600` - `supporting`: no border, text `--color-elephant-500` - `minor`: no border, text `--color-cement-400` - Mention count badge: `{mentionCount} mention{s}` in `--font-size-xs`, `--color-cement-400` — hidden if 0 to avoid noise - Aliases count: `{n} alias{es}` in `--font-size-xs`, `--color-cement-400` — hidden if aliases array is empty - `isSelected` state: `--color-fog-200` background on the list item - `onClick` on the entire item; `tabIndex={0}`, keyboard `Enter`/`Space` triggers click --- ### Step 5 — `CharacterDetail` component `src/components/CharacterBible/CharacterDetail.tsx` Props: ```ts interface CharacterDetailProps { character: Character; mentionEntry: MentionEntry | undefined; onUpdate: (updates: Partial>) => void; onDelete: () => void; } ``` Layout: scrollable right panel, `320px` on desktop, `100%` mobile (`@media (max-width: 767px)`). Sections, top to bottom: 1. **Header row**: initials avatar (same as CharacterCard, `48px`) + name input (editable `` inline, `font-size: var(--font-size-xl)`, `--font-weight-semibold`) + role `` with placeholder "e.g. Alex, Al, Alexander — comma separated". On blur: split by comma, trim whitespace, filter empty strings, call `onUpdate({ aliases: [...] })`. 3. **Description**: `

title: Link worldbuilding entities inline in the editor with mention chips and quick peek
description:
## Problem
Writers track characters, places, and lore in separate panels, but references inside prose are not structurally linked. This creates continuity drift and forces manual cross-checking during revision.

## Target user
Fiction writers managing recurring characters/locations/rules who need context while drafting without leaving the editor.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase usage of linked worldbuilding entities per manuscript
- Reduce continuity-related revisions tied to character/location inconsistency
- Increase frequency of worldbuilding panel interactions during drafting sessions

## Scope
- Add inline mention trigger in editor (e.g., @) to insert linked entity chips for character/location/rule items
- Store and render mentions as durable links to worldbuilding records
- Add hover/click quick peek card showing key entity details without full panel switch
- Provide mention management in scene metadata or inspector (list all mentions in current scene)
- Keep plain-text export fallback by converting mention chips to readable text labels

## Non-goals
- Automatic NER/entity detection from raw prose
- Complex bi-directional graph analytics in v1
- Multi-user mention resolution workflows

## Acceptance criteria
- User can insert entity mentions from the editor via keyboard and mouse
- Mention chips render consistently after reload and scene switches
- Clicking/hovering a mention opens a lightweight entity peek with core fields
- Removing an entity from worldbuilding does not corrupt manuscript text
- Export output remains readable when mentions are present

## Priority rationale
Campfire demonstrates demand for strong world context, but often in separate modules. Inline linking keeps the writer in flow and can become a differentiation point: context-rich drafting without UI sprawl.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

## Goal

Replace the plain `<textarea>` in the story editor with a `contenteditable` div that supports inline mention chips for worldbuilding entities (characters, locations, rules). Writers trigger the insertion flow by typing `@`, select from a floating command menu, and get a durable inline chip that survives saves, reloads, and entity renames. A hover/tap quick peek card surfaces entity details without leaving the writing flow. The right sidebar converts its "Notes" placeholder to a "Scene mentions" list.

---

## Scope and files

### New files

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/types/worldbuilding.ts` | `WorldbuildingEntity` type and entity type union |
| `src/lib/worldbuilding.ts` | `getWorldbuildingEntities`, `getWorldbuildingEntity` — localStorage read helpers |
| `src/lib/mentions.ts` | Serialize/deserialize mention tokens; plain-text export; extract mentions from encoded content |
| `src/components/MentionEditor/MentionEditor.tsx` | `contenteditable` editor component with `@`-trigger logic |
| `src/components/MentionEditor/MentionEditor.module.css` | Editor + chip + focus-ring styles |
| `src/components/MentionMenu/MentionMenu.tsx` | Floating command menu portal — keyboard-navigable entity picker |
| `src/components/MentionMenu/MentionMenu.module.css` | Menu, group header, item, highlight styles |
| `src/components/MentionPeek/MentionPeek.tsx` | Hover/tap quick peek popover portal |
| `src/components/MentionPeek/MentionPeek.module.css` | Peek card styles |
| `src/components/SceneMentions/SceneMentions.tsx` | Sidebar panel: mentions grouped by type with counts |
| `src/components/SceneMentions/SceneMentions.module.css` | Sidebar panel styles |

### Modified files

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` | Swap `<textarea>` for `<MentionEditor>`; replace sidebar "Notes" placeholder with `<SceneMentions>`; thread `storyId` and serialized content through both |
| `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` | Remove `textarea`-specific rules from `.editor`; add `.editorSurface` rules for contenteditable (same font, padding, line-height) |

---

## Step-by-step plan

### Step 1 — Define the WorldbuildingEntity type

**File: `src/types/worldbuilding.ts`**

```ts
export type EntityType = 'character' | 'location' | 'rule';

export interface WorldbuildingEntity {
  id: string;           // crypto.randomUUID()
  storyId: string;
  type: EntityType;
  name: string;
  fields: Record<string, string>; // e.g. { role: 'Protagonist', age: '32' }
  createdAt: number;
  updatedAt: number;
}
```

The `fields` map is open-ended; quick peek renders the first 2-3 entries. This matches the BIN-66 worldbuilding panel contract.

---

### Step 2 — Create the worldbuilding lib

**File: `src/lib/worldbuilding.ts`**

localStorage key convention: `story_${storyId}_worldbuilding` — stores a `WorldbuildingEntity[]` JSON array, matching the pattern in `src/lib/scenes.ts`.

```ts
export function getWorldbuildingEntities(storyId: string): WorldbuildingEntity[]
export function getWorldbuildingEntity(storyId: string, entityId: string): WorldbuildingEntity | null
```

Both are pure reads — BIN-66 owns writes. `getWorldbuildingEntities` returns `[]` safely on parse error or missing key, using the same defensive `try/catch` pattern from `stories.ts`.

---

### Step 3 — Define the content encoding and mention lib

**File: `src/lib/mentions.ts`**

#### Token format

Mentions are encoded inline in the plain-text content string as:

```
[[@DisplayName|entityType|entityId]]
```

Example: `[[@Elena Vasquez|character|abc-123]]`

Rules:
- `DisplayName` is the entity name at time of insertion (serves as human-readable fallback).
- `entityType` is one of `character | location | rule`.
- `entityId` is the stable UUID.
- The token delimiter `[[@...]]` is chosen to be invisible in normal prose and unambiguous for regex parsing.

#### Functions to implement

```ts
// Parse a raw content string into segments
export type ContentSegment =
  | { kind: 'text'; value: string }
  | { kind: 'mention'; displayName: string; entityType: EntityType; entityId: string };

export function parseContent(raw: string): ContentSegment[]

// Serialize segments back to the storage string
export function serializeContent(segments: ContentSegment[]): string

// Extract all mention tokens from a raw content string (for SceneMentions sidebar)
export function extractMentions(raw: string): Array<{ displayName: string; entityType: EntityType; entityId: string }>

// Export-safe plain text: replace all tokens with their displayName
export function toPlainText(raw: string): string
```

`parseContent` uses a single `RegExp` — `/\[\[@([^|]+)\|([^|]+)\|([^\]]+)\]\]/g` — split via `matchAll`. No external parser needed.

---

### Step 4 — Build MentionEditor (core contenteditable component)

**File: `src/components/MentionEditor/MentionEditor.tsx`**

#### Interface

```ts
interface MentionEditorProps {
  storyId: string;
  value: string;           // encoded content string (tokens + plain text)
  onChange: (encoded: string) => void;
  placeholder?: string;
  id?: string;
  'aria-label'?: string;
}
```

`value`/`onChange` use the same encoded string format as `autosave.ts` and `useAutosave`. No change to the autosave pipeline.

#### Internal state

```ts
const editorRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
const [menuState, setMenuState] = useState<{
  open: boolean;
  query: string;
  anchorRect: DOMRect | null;
  triggerOffset: number;   // character offset of '@' in the raw text being composed
} | null>(null);
const [peekState, setPeekState] = useState<{
  entityId: string;
  anchorRect: DOMRect;
} | null>(null);
```

#### Rendering logic (controlled contenteditable)

The `value` prop is the source of truth. On each render, MentionEditor converts it to DOM content using `parseContent`. Each mention segment becomes a `<span data-mention-id="{id}" data-mention-type="{type}" contenteditable="false" tabindex="0">` chip. Text segments become plain text nodes.

Use `useEffect` to synchronize the DOM only when the encoded `value` differs from what is currently serialized from the DOM — compare via `serializeContent(domToSegments(editorRef.current))`. This avoids cursor-reset on every keystroke.

DO NOT use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` on every render. Instead, on mount and only when `value` changes externally (e.g., autosave restore), do a full DOM rebuild via a dedicated `rebuildDOM(div, segments)` helper.

#### Keyboard handling — `onKeyDown` on the div

- **`@` key pressed**: Record caret offset via `window.getSelection()`, set `menuState.open = true`, `menuState.query = ''`, compute `anchorRect` from `selection.getRangeAt(0).getBoundingClientRect()`.
- **While menu open**: Arrow Up/Down navigate menu; Enter inserts; Esc closes menu.
- **Backspace on a chip**: If the caret is immediately after a chip (`contenteditable="false"` span), remove the chip and update `onChange`.

#### `onInput` handler

During normal typing (no menu open): read `innerText`, diff against prior state, call `onChange(serializeDOMToEncoded(editorRef.current))`.

While menu open: capture the characters typed after `@` to update `menuState.query`.

#### Chip click/hover

- `onMouseEnter` on a chip span: compute `getBoundingClientRect()`, set `peekState`.
- `onClick` (or `onKeyDown` Enter/Space) on a chip: set `peekState`.
- `onMouseLeave` on chip / Esc global keydown: clear `peekState`.

#### `onChange` propagation

After any DOM mutation that changes content, call:
```ts
onChange(serializeDOMToEncoded(editorRef.current));
```
This keeps `useAutosave` receiving encoded strings unchanged — autosave stores tokens verbatim and restores them correctly.

---

### Step 5 — Build MentionMenu (floating command menu)

**File: `src/components/MentionMenu/MentionMenu.tsx`**

Rendered via `ReactDOM.createPortal` into `document.body`.

#### Interface

```ts
interface MentionMenuProps {
  storyId: string;
  query: string;
  anchorRect: DOMRect;
  onSelect: (entity: WorldbuildingEntity) => void;
  onClose: () => void;
}
```

#### Positioning

```ts
const style = {
  position: 'fixed',
  top: anchorRect.bottom + 4,   // 4px gap below caret
  left: anchorRect.left,
  zIndex: 'var(--z-dropdown)',  // 1000
};
```

Clamp `left` so the menu never overflows the viewport right edge: `Math.min(left, window.innerWidth - MENU_WIDTH - 8)`. Flip above caret if `anchorRect.bottom + MENU_HEIGHT > window.innerHeight`.

#### Content

- Load entities with `getWorldbuildingEntities(storyId)` on each open.
- Filter by `query` (case-insensitive substring match on `entity.name`).
- Group results by `entity.type` with group headers: "Characters", "Locations", "Rules".
- Max 8 results total (truncate with "…more" note if exceeded).
- Keyboard: `activeIndex` state; ArrowDown/Up wrap; Enter fires `onSelect`; Esc fires `onClose`.
- Empty state: "No entities match '@{query}'" in muted text.

#### Accessibility

- `role="listbox"`, `aria-label="Insert worldbuilding mention"`.
- Each item: `role="option"`, `aria-selected={activeIndex === i}`.
- Focus stays in the editor div; arrow key events are intercepted before reaching the div.

---

### Step 6 — Build MentionPeek (quick peek popover)

**File: `src/components/MentionPeek/MentionPeek.tsx`**

Rendered via `ReactDOM.createPortal` into `document.body`.

#### Interface

```ts
interface MentionPeekProps {
  storyId: string;
  entityId: string;
  anchorRect: DOMRect;
  onClose: () => void;
}
```

#### Content

Calls `getWorldbuildingEntity(storyId, entityId)`. If `null` (deleted entity):
- Title: "Missing entity"
- Body: "This entity has been removed from your worldbuilding notes."
- No "Open full details" link.

If found:
- Entity name (bold), type badge (small all-caps label).
- Up to 3 key fields from `entity.fields` rendered as `label: value` pairs.
- "Open full details" button (currently `console.log` — wired to worldbuilding panel when BIN-66 is available).

#### Positioning

Same viewport-clamping logic as MentionMenu. Open above chip if not enough space below. Use `--z-popover: 1400`.

#### Accessibility

- `role="tooltip"` (or `role="dialog"` if interactive — use `dialog` since it has a button).
- `aria-label="Entity details"`.
- Closed on `Escape` global keydown, click outside, or `onMouseLeave` of both chip and peek card (use a short `setTimeout` of 150ms to allow mouse travel between chip and card).

---

### Step 7 — Build SceneMentions sidebar panel

**File: `src/components/SceneMentions/SceneMentions.tsx`**

#### Interface

```ts
interface SceneMentionsProps {
  storyId: string;
  encodedContent: string;
}
```

#### Logic

1. `const mentions = extractMentions(encodedContent)` — get all mention tokens in the current content.
2. Deduplicate by `entityId`, count occurrences.
3. Resolve each via `getWorldbuildingEntity` — mark unresolved as "deleted".
4. Group by `entityType`: Characters, Locations, Rules.
5. Render each group as a collapsible section (use `<details>`/`<summary>` — no extra state needed).

Each mention item shows:
- Entity name (or "Deleted entity" in muted style for missing ones).
- Occurrence count as a small badge.
- Clicking an item opens the quick peek card (inline inline version, not the hover popover — pass `onPeekRequest` callback up or manage `peekState` locally).

Empty state: "No entity mentions yet. Type @ to insert one." in muted text.

---

### Step 8 — Update page.tsx

**File: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`**

Two surgical changes in the `viewMode === 'editor'` block:

**Change A — Replace textarea with MentionEditor**

Remove:
```tsx
<label htmlFor="story-editor" className={styles.visuallyHidden}>
  Story content editor
</label>
<textarea
  id="story-editor"
  className={styles.editor}
  placeholder="Start writing your story..."
  aria-label="Story content editor"
  value={content}
  onChange={(e) => setContent(e.target.value)}
/>
```

Add:
```tsx
<MentionEditor
  id="story-editor"
  storyId={id}
  value={content}
  onChange={setContent}
  placeholder="Start writing your story..."
  aria-label="Story content editor"
/>
```

`content` and `setContent` come from `useAutosave` unchanged — the encoded string flows through the same autosave pipeline.

**Change B — Replace sidebar Notes placeholder with SceneMentions**

Remove the entire `sidebarSection` div containing the "Notes" heading and `sidebarPlaceholder`.

Add:
```tsx
<SceneMentions storyId={id} encodedContent={content} />
```

---

### Step 9 — Update page.module.css

Remove `textarea`-specific properties from `.editor` (the `resize: none` rule, `value`-based sizing). Add `.editorSurface` or reuse `.editor` class on the `contenteditable` div with:

```css
.editor {
  /* existing: width, min-height, padding, font-family, font-size, line-height, color, background, border, outline */
  cursor: text;
  white-space: pre-wrap;
  word-break: break-word;
  overflow-wrap: break-word;
}
```

Add chip styles in `MentionEditor.module.css` (not in page.module.css):

```css
.chip {
  display: inline-block;
  background-color: var(--color-polar-100);
  color: var(--color-carbon-600);
  border: 1px solid var(--color-fog-200);
  border-radius: var(--border-radius-sm);
  padding: 0 var(--spacing-xs);
  font-size: var(--font-size-sm);
  font-family: var(--font-sans);    /* override serif editor font for chips */
  line-height: 1.4;
  vertical-align: baseline;
  cursor: pointer;
  user-select: none;
}

.chip:hover {
  border-color: var(--color-elephant-500);
}

.chip:focus {
  outline: 2px solid var(--color-carbon-600);
  outline-offset: 2px;
}

.chipMissing {
  color: var(--color-cement-400);
  background-color: var(--color-fog-200);
  border-color: var(--color-fog-200);
}
```

---

### Step 10 — Export plain-text utility integration

No new integration point needed at this step — `toPlainText(raw: string)` from `mentions.ts` is available for any future export feature. Document it in a comment in `mentions.ts` as the export entry point.

If the story page ever serializes for copy-to-clipboard or "Export TXT", the caller does: `toPlainText(content)` before writing.

---

## Test plan

### Unit tests (manual verification sufficient for v1 — document expected behaviors)

**`src/lib/mentions.ts`**

| Test | Expected |
|---|---|
| `parseContent('Hello [[@Elena|character|id1]] went to [[@Tower|location|id2]].')` | Returns 5 segments: text, mention, text, mention, text |
| `serializeContent(parseContent(raw))` | Round-trips identically to `raw` |
| `toPlainText(raw)` | Returns `'Hello Elena went to Tower.'` |
| `extractMentions(raw)` | Returns 2 items with correct ids and types |
| Malformed token `[[@broken]]` | Treated as plain text — no crash |
| Empty string | Returns `[{ kind: 'text', value: '' }]` |

**`src/lib/worldbuilding.ts`**

| Test | Expected |
|---|---|
| `getWorldbuildingEntities(storyId)` when key missing | Returns `[]` |
| `getWorldbuildingEntity(storyId, 'nonexistent')` | Returns `null` |

### Component integration tests (manual browser walkthrough)

**MentionEditor — insertion flow**

1. Open a story in editor mode.
2. Click into the editor, type "Once upon a time ".
3. Type `@El` — menu opens, shows matching entities.
4. Arrow-down to select, press Enter — chip inserts at caret, menu closes.
5. Continue typing after chip — prose continues normally.
6. Save page (wait 2s for autosave debounce), reload — chip renders correctly.
7. Inspect localStorage `story_${id}_content` — verify encoded token is present.

**MentionEditor — keyboard navigation**

1. Type `@` — menu opens.
2. Arrow Down x2 — third item highlighted.
3. Esc — menu closes, `@` character remains (user can continue typing or delete it).
4. Tab past a chip — chip receives focus ring.
5. Enter on focused chip — quick peek opens.
6. Esc — peek closes.

**MentionEditor — backspace removal**

1. Place caret immediately after a chip.
2. Press Backspace — chip is removed in full (not partially deleted as characters).
3. `onChange` fires with chip token absent from encoded string.

**MentionMenu — empty state**

1. Type `@zzz` — no entities match — menu shows "No entities match '@zzz'".
2. Backspace to `@` — all entities listed again.

**MentionPeek — deleted entity**

1. Insert a mention chip for an entity.
2. Manually remove the entity from localStorage (simulate BIN-66 delete).
3. Reload page — chip renders with `.chipMissing` style and "Missing entity" label.
4. Hover chip — peek card shows "This entity has been removed…" text, no crash.

**SceneMentions sidebar**

1. Insert 3 mentions: 2 characters, 1 location.
2. Sidebar updates immediately to show "Characters (2)", "Locations (1)".
3. Delete one character chip — sidebar updates to "Characters (1)".
4. No mentions — sidebar shows empty state text.

**Autosave compatibility**

1. Type content with a mention chip.
2. Wait for autosave (2s debounce + "Saved X ago" confirmation).
3. Reload page.
4. Verify content is identical to pre-reload (encoded token preserved).
5. Trigger crash recovery: make a change, close tab before save, reopen — RecoveryBanner appears, click Restore — chip is present.

**Export plain text**

1. Open browser console.
2. Run `import('/lib/mentions').then(m => console.log(m.toPlainText(localStorage.getItem('story_${id}_content'))))`.
3. Verify output contains entity names as plain text, no token syntax.

**Accessibility audit**

1. Tab through editor, chips, and menu — verify visible focus rings everywhere.
2. Screen reader (VoiceOver): chips announce as "Elena Vasquez, character mention".
3. MentionMenu has `role="listbox"` — announcements work on arrow key navigation.
4. MentionPeek `role="dialog"` — focus trap is NOT applied (it's a non-modal tooltip-like card); Esc closes it.

---

## Risks and mitigations

| Risk | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Caret position lost after controlled DOM rebuild | High | Compare serialized DOM vs incoming `value` before rebuilding; only rebuild on external changes, not on user keystrokes |
| IME (Chinese/Japanese/Korean) input fires `onInput` mid-composition and corrupts tokens | Medium | Use `compositionstart` / `compositionend` events to gate `onInput` handling; skip serialization while composing |
| Paste event inserts raw HTML with styling | Medium | Intercept `onPaste`, extract `clipboardData.getData('text/plain')`, insert as plain text only |
| Chip spanning line break causes unexpected selection behavior | Low | Chips are `inline-block` with `contenteditable="false"` — browser treats them atomically; test in Firefox/Safari |
| `getBoundingClientRect()` on caret returns zero rect (collapsed range edge case) | Low | Fall back to `editorRef.current.getBoundingClientRect()` (position menu at top-left of editor) |
| `getWorldbuildingEntities` returns stale data after BIN-66 adds new entities | Low | MentionMenu calls `getWorldbuildingEntities` on every menu open (not cached in state) — always fresh |
| Large documents (10k+ words) with many chips cause slow DOM rebuild | Low | Rebuild only fires on external `value` changes; user keystrokes use incremental DOM mutation, not rebuild |
| Mention token format collides with user-typed text `[[@...]]` | Very Low | Unlikely in normal prose; document the collision in `mentions.ts` as a known v1 limitation |

---

## Rollback strategy

The `<textarea>` is replaced with `<MentionEditor>` in a single JSX block in `page.tsx`. If a critical defect is found post-merge:

1. Revert `page.tsx` to restore the original `<textarea>` block and original sidebar.
2. The encoded content strings already saved to localStorage are still valid as plain text — any `[[@...]]` tokens are visible as literal text in the textarea, which is readable and not corrupting.
3. The new files (`MentionEditor`, `MentionMenu`, `MentionPeek`, `SceneMentions`, `mentions.ts`, `worldbuilding.ts`) can remain in the codebase without effect since nothing imports them after the revert.
4. Autosave (`useAutosave`, `autosave.ts`) is untouched — no rollback needed there.

---

## Observability

All logging follows the existing `console.log` / `console.error` pattern in `scenes.ts` and `autosave.ts`.

Add the following log points:

| Location | Log |
|---|---|
| `MentionEditor` — on chip insertion | `console.log('Mention inserted:', entityId, entityType)` |
| `MentionEditor` — on chip removal | `console.log('Mention removed:', entityId)` |
| `worldbuilding.ts` — `getWorldbuildingEntities` parse error | `console.error('Failed to parse worldbuilding entities:', error)` |
| `mentions.ts` — `parseContent` encountering malformed token | `console.warn('Skipped malformed mention token:', raw)` |
| `MentionPeek` — entity not found (deleted) | `console.warn('Mention peek: entity not found:', entityId)` |

No metrics, no error tracking service — consistent with v1 approach across the codebase.

---

## Acceptance mapping

| Acceptance criterion | How it is addressed |
|---|---|
| User can insert entity mentions from the editor via keyboard and mouse | `@` trigger → MentionMenu → Enter (keyboard) or click (mouse) → chip inserted |
| Mention chips render consistently after reload and scene switches | Content stored as encoded string in localStorage via existing autosave; `MentionEditor` rebuilds DOM from encoded string on mount |
| Clicking/hovering a mention opens a lightweight entity peek with core fields | `MentionPeek` portal renders on chip hover (desktop) and click/Enter (mobile/keyboard) |
| Removing an entity from worldbuilding does not corrupt manuscript text | `getWorldbuildingEntity` returns `null` for deleted entities; chip renders with `.chipMissing` style and peek shows "Missing entity" — prose selection and editing remain unaffected |
| Export output remains readable when mentions are present | `toPlainText(raw)` in `mentions.ts` replaces all tokens with their `displayName`; backward-compatible since display name is embedded in the token at insertion time |

---

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey

</plan>

Build persistent Story Library with create, rename, archive, and resume
description: ## Problem The app currently shows empty placeholders and does not let writers manage real stories. Without persistent story records and quick resume, users cannot build a daily writing habit or trust the app for ongoing projects. ## Target user Solo fiction writers drafting one or more long-form stories who need a reliable home screen and library to manage works-in-progress. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase weekly active writers who return to an existing story - At least 70% of writing sessions start from Quick Resume or Stories list after launch - Reduce first-session drop-off between story creation and first edit ## Scope - Add story persistence layer for metadata (title, updatedAt, archive flag) - Stories page supports create, rename, archive/unarchive, and delete-confirmation guardrail - Home dashboard shows Recent Stories and Quick Resume based on last edited items - Clicking any story opens `/story/[id]` for editing - Empty states include clear primary actions (Create story / Restore archived) ## Non-goals - Real-time multi-user collaboration - Publishing/sharing workflows - AI writing assistance ## Acceptance criteria - User can create a new story and see it appear in Stories and Home Recent list immediately - Renaming a story persists across reload and navigation - Archived stories are hidden from default list and recoverable from an archive filter - Quick Resume always points to most recently edited non-archived story - Deleting requires explicit confirmation and removes the story from all lists ## Priority rationale This is the minimum product surface required to make the app usable beyond a demo. It unlocks all downstream drafting and structure features by making stories first-class persisted objects. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Build a persistent Story Library that lets writers create, rename, archive, and resume stories with a resume-first Home dashboard, enabling daily writing habits and multi-project management. ## Scope and files **New files:** - `src/types/story.ts` - Story data model (title, id, updatedAt, isArchived) - `src/lib/stories.ts` - Story CRUD functions with localStorage persistence - `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.tsx` - Story card/row component for lists - `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.module.css` - Story card styles - `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx` - Reusable confirmation modal - `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.module.css` - Dialog styles - `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.tsx` - Inline action menu (rename, archive, delete) - `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.module.css` - Menu styles **Modified files:** - `src/app/page.tsx` - Wire up Quick Resume and Recent Stories with real data - `src/app/page.module.css` - Add styles for Resume button and story rows - `src/app/stories/page.tsx` - Add New Story button, story list, archive toggle - `src/app/stories/page.module.css` - Add toolbar button and list grid styles ## Step-by-step plan ### 1. Define Story data model and persistence layer Create `src/types/story.ts`: - Export `Story` interface with `id: string`, `title: string`, `updatedAt: number` (timestamp), `isArchived: boolean`, `createdAt: number` - Keep model minimal (no content field yet, only metadata) Create `src/lib/stories.ts`: - `getStories(): Story[]` - Load from localStorage `stories` key, return sorted by `updatedAt` desc - `getStory(id: string): Story | null` - Get single story by id - `createStory(title: string): Story` - Generate UUID, set timestamps, persist, return story - `updateStory(id: string, updates: Partial>): Story | null` - Merge updates, update `updatedAt`, persist - `deleteStory(id: string): void` - Remove from array, persist - All functions handle localStorage errors gracefully (try/catch with console.error) - Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for id generation - Storage key: `stories` ### 2. Build ConfirmDialog component Create `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx`: - Props: `isOpen: boolean`, `title: string`, `message: string`, `confirmLabel: string`, `onConfirm: () => void`, `onCancel: () => void`, `destructive?: boolean` - Render modal overlay with dialog (use semantic `` element or ARIA role) - Focus Cancel button by default (safe default) - ESC key triggers cancel - Backdrop click triggers cancel - Style with grayscale tokens, `--shadow-lg`, `--z-modal` Create `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.module.css`: - Overlay: fixed, full viewport, dark semi-transparent backdrop - Dialog: centered, white background, padding, border-radius-md, shadow-lg - Buttons: inline-flex, spacing-md gap - Destructive variant uses darker text for confirm button ### 3. Build StoryActionMenu component Create `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.tsx`: - Props: `storyId: string`, `storyTitle: string`, `isArchived: boolean`, `onUpdate: () => void` - State: `menuOpen: boolean`, `renameDialogOpen: boolean`, `deleteDialogOpen: boolean`, `newTitle: string` - Render overflow button (three dots or "···") that toggles menu - Menu options (conditional): - **Rename** (always): Opens inline prompt or dialog with text input pre-filled with current title - **Archive/Unarchive** (toggle based on `isArchived`): Calls `updateStory` immediately, closes menu, calls `onUpdate` - **Delete** (always): Opens ConfirmDialog with story title in message, on confirm calls `deleteStory`, calls `onUpdate` - Close menu on option select or outside click - Keyboard: menu items navigable with arrow keys, Enter to select, ESC to close Create `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.module.css`: - Menu button: icon button, hover state - Dropdown: absolute positioned, white bg, shadow-md, border - Menu items: block links/buttons, hover bg fog-200 ### 4. Build StoryCard component Create `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.tsx`: - Props: `story: Story`, `showActions?: boolean`, `onUpdate?: () => void`, `onClick?: () => void` - Render card with: - Story title (h4 or similar, clickable if `onClick` provided) - Last edited timestamp (formatted as "Last edited X hours/days ago" using relative time helper or hardcoded formatter) - Action menu (if `showActions` is true and `onUpdate` provided) - Entire card clickable if `onClick` provided (use keyboard-accessible button wrapper) - Focus state with 2px outline (--color-fashion-300) Create `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.module.css`: - Card: border, border-radius-md, padding-md, hover state (shadow-sm elevation) - Desktop: 2-column grid support via parent - Mobile: full-width stack - Typography: title uses --font-size-lg, timestamp uses --font-size-sm with --color-cement-400 ### 5. Update Home page for Quick Resume and Recent Stories Modify `src/app/page.tsx`: - Import `getStories` from `@/lib/stories` - Load stories in component (client component with `useState` + `useEffect` or Server Component if no interactivity needed; choose client for future optimistic updates) - Filter non-archived stories, sort by `updatedAt` desc - **Quick Resume card:** - If stories exist, show most recent (index 0): title, "Last edited X ago", and primary button "Resume writing" that navigates to `/story/[id]` - If no stories, show current empty state - **Recent Stories card:** - If stories exist, render list (max 5) of `StoryCard` with `onClick` navigating to `/story/[id]` and `showActions={true}` with `onUpdate` triggering re-fetch - If no stories, show current empty state - Add `router.push` navigation using `next/navigation` `useRouter` Modify `src/app/page.module.css`: - Add styles for `.resumeButton` (primary CTA: background --color-black-700, color white, padding, border-radius-md) - Add styles for `.storyList` (vertical stack with spacing-sm gap) - Add styles for `.timestamp` (--font-size-sm, --color-cement-400) ### 6. Update Stories page for create/list/archive Modify `src/app/stories/page.tsx`: - Convert to client component (`'use client'`) - Import `getStories`, `createStory` from `@/lib/stories` - State: `stories: Story[]`, `showArchived: boolean`, `isCreating: boolean` - `useEffect` to load stories on mount - Computed: `filteredStories = stories.filter(s => s.isArchived === showArchived)` - **Toolbar:** - Left: `New Story` button → calls `createStory("Untitled Story")`, navigates to `/story/[newId]`, sets `isCreating` loading state - Center: keep existing search input (not wired yet, placeholder for future) - Right: Archive toggle button (e.g., "Show Archived" / "Hide Archived") toggles `showArchived` - **Stories list:** - If `filteredStories.length > 0`, render grid/list of `StoryCard` with `onClick` and `showActions` - If empty and `!showArchived`, show "No stories yet" with "Create your first story to get started" - If empty and `showArchived`, show "No archived stories" with "Archive a story to see it here" - On story update (archive/rename/delete), refetch stories with `getStories()` Modify `src/app/stories/page.module.css`: - Add `.newStoryButton` (button styles, left-aligned in toolbar) - Add `.archiveToggle` (toggle button, right-aligned) - Add `.storiesGrid` (desktop: 2-column grid with gap, mobile: 1-column) - Add `.emptyState` refinements if needed (already exists, may need minor tweaks) ### 7. Wire story navigation In `StoryCard` and Home/Stories pages: - Use `next/navigation` `useRouter().push('/story/' + story.id)` for navigation - Ensure `/story/[id]/page.tsx` can handle any valid UUID id (it currently does) ### 8. Add accessibility and keyboard support - All interactive elements must be keyboard-navigable (buttons, menu items, dialogs) - Focus management: dialog opens → focus Cancel, dialog closes → return focus to trigger - ARIA: dialog has `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby` for title, `aria-describedby` for message - Screen reader: action menu announces "Actions for [story title]" - Test tab order: toolbar → story cards → action menus ### 9. Test responsive: desktop 2-col, tablet/mobile 1-col - Test keyboard: tab through all controls, activate with Enter/Space ## Test plan **Unit/integration tests (manual):** 1. **Create story:** - Click "New Story" on Stories page - Verify navigates to `/story/[id]` with new UUID - Go back, verify story appears in Stories list and Home Recent Stories 2. **Rename story:** - Click action menu on a story → Rename - Enter new title, confirm - Verify title updates in both Stories list and Home - Reload page, verify persistence 3. **Archive story:** - Click action menu → Archive - Verify story disappears from default list - Toggle "Show Archived", verify story appears - Reload, verify deletion persisted 5. **Quick Resume:** - Create/edit multiple stories - Verify Home shows most recently edited story in Quick Resume - Click "Resume writing", verify navigates to correct story 6. **Empty states:** - Delete all stories - Verify Home shows "No stories yet" / "Nothing in progress" - Verify Stories shows "No stories yet" with create CTA - Toggle archive, verify "No archived stories" 7. **Persistence:** - Open DevTools → Application → Local Storage - Verify `stories` key exists with JSON array - Manually corrupt JSON, reload, verify graceful error handling (empty list) **Accessibility tests:** - Tab through entire Home and Stories pages, verify all buttons/menus reachable - Open action menu, navigate with arrow keys, select with Enter - Open delete dialog, press ESC, verify closes - Test with VoiceOver/NVDA: verify announcements for action menu, dialog **Responsive tests:** - Desktop (1024px+): 2-column story cards, single-row toolbar - Tablet (768-1023px): 1-column stories, toolbar may wrap - Mobile (<768px): 1-column, full-width buttons **Commands:** ```bash npm run dev # Manual testing in browser at http://localhost:3000 # Test all CRUD operations, navigation, persistence, accessibility ``` ## Risks and mitigations **Risk 1: Data loss due to localStorage limits or corruption** - Mitigation: Wrap all localStorage operations in try/catch, log errors to console - Future: Add localStorage quota check, warn user if nearing limit - Mitigation: Keep story metadata small (no content in library layer yet) **Risk 2: Race conditions with rapid create/update/delete** - Mitigation: Keep state simple: single `stories` array, re-fetch on any mutation - Mitigation: Use `useEffect` dependencies carefully to avoid infinite loops - Future: Consider React Query or SWR for caching if performance issues arise **Risk 4: Accessibility gaps in custom dialog/menu components** -
