## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This moves us from "drafting tool" to "submission-ready workflow". Competitors emphasize writing structure, worldbuilding, or reader community; a fast, integrated submission package builder is a concrete endgame advantage for writers preparing outreach.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter’s pricing/features highlight plotting and export capabilities (e.g., Advanced Plot Board, export formats), but not a guided submission-package workflow (query + synopsis + beta packet): https://livingwriter.com/pricing
- Campfire’s feature positioning emphasizes modules for planning/worldbuilding (story outlines, interactive maps, worldbuilding tools), not manuscript-to-submission packaging: https://www.campfirewriting.com/interactive-maps
- Inkitt’s writer guidance centers on reader growth/community engagement and platform presence, indicating a different value focus than submission prep tooling: https://www.inkitt.com/writersblog/inkitt-author-/

## Risks / trade-offs
- Scope creep risk if this expands into CRM/emailing workflows.
- Template quality must balance flexibility with enough guidance for novice submitters.
- Export fidelity (DOCX + plain text) can become a support burden if formatting is inconsistent.

## Recommendation
Approve with a design brief that prioritizes speed-to-first-package (<5 minutes), low-friction metadata refresh UX, and clear status/version semantics so submission prep feels reliable rather than heavyweight.

## Handoff

[Design]:
## UX goal
Enable a writer to create, edit, and export a complete submission package in under 5 minutes from the story workspace, with a flow that feels as direct as drafting.

## Information hierarchy
1. Story context: package title, current status (`draft`, `ready`, `submitted`), last updated timestamp.
2. Core actions: `Create package`, `Refresh metadata`, `Export DOCX bundle`, `Copy plain text`.
3. Document workspace: Query Letter, One-Page Synopsis, Long Synopsis, Beta-Reader Briefing as first-class editable tabs/cards.
4. Version history: chronological list of saved versions with status badges and restore/view affordance.
5. Supporting metadata panel: title, genre, logline, comps, protagonist/conflict placeholders with clear source-of-truth messaging.

## Layout and components
- Add a story-level mode switch option for `Submission Package` alongside existing `Editor`/`Corkboard` patterns (same one-click interaction model).
- Use a two-column desktop layout aligned to current story page composition:
  - Main column: selected document editor with template scaffold and inline section headings.
  - Right rail: package status card, metadata snapshot, version history list, export actions.
- On first entry, show a focused “Create submission package” setup panel (single CTA + lightweight checklist).
- Document navigation should use a compact segmented/tab row (4 document types) anchored above the editor body.
- Reuse existing card/button/input visual language from stories and story page headers; avoid introducing novel control styles.

## Interaction states
- Idle:
  - Existing package shows current document content immediately and active document tab.
  - `Refresh metadata` is available with a subtle “last synced” label.
- Loading:
  - Skeleton rows for version list and placeholder blocks in editor body.
  - Export buttons show disabled loading text (`Preparing export…`).
- Empty:
  - No package yet: single callout card with 3-step explanation (generate, edit, export) and one primary CTA.
  - Empty version history: helper copy “Versions appear after first save.”
- Error:
  - Inline banner in main column (non-modal) with plain-language failure and retry action.
  - Export failures should preserve current edits and show retry without page reset.
- Success:
  - Non-blocking confirmation toast/inline notice after save, metadata refresh, and export.
  - Version creation confirms timestamp + status badge added to history.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard:
  - Full tab order: mode switch -> document tabs -> editor -> metadata actions -> version list.
  - Arrow key support for document tablist; Enter/Space activates actions.
- Focus:
  - Reuse current high-contrast focus outlines (`--color-carbon-600`) on all interactive controls.
  - Do not trap focus except in explicit confirmation dialog for metadata refresh.
- Contrast:
  - Keep text/background combinations at AA minimum using existing grayscale tokens.
  - Status badges need text contrast distinct from surrounding card backgrounds.
- Text clarity:
  - Use direct writer-facing labels (“Query letter”, “Long synopsis”) and avoid system jargon.

## Visual style
- Follow existing grayscale system in `src/styles/tokens.css`; use only approved tokens (`--color-black-700` through `--color-polar-100`, `--color-white`).
- Preserve current calm, document-first tone: clean surfaces, subtle borders, no saturated accents.
- Typography should match app hierarchy:
  - Page/document headers: semibold sans token scale.
  - Body writing fields: serif editor treatment consistent with current story editor.
- Use status chips with grayscale differentiation (border + fill + label) rather than color-only signaling.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024px): two-column editing workspace (main editor + right rail) with sticky top action strip.
- Tablet (768-1023px): collapse right rail below editor as stacked cards; keep document tabs fixed at top of content.
- Mobile (<768px): single-column flow; tabs become horizontally scrollable pills; actions prioritize `Save` and `Export` with secondary options under overflow.
- Maintain one-tap mode switching and avoid deep nesting/modals on mobile.

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed architecture and primitives:
  - `src/app/layout.tsx`
  - `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`
  - `src/components/Navigation/Navigation.tsx`
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` + `page.module.css`
  - `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`
  - `src/styles/tokens.css`
- Recommendation is intentionally aligned to current story-route-centered UX and CSS-module token usage.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - `ViewModeSwitch` interaction model for one-click mode changes.
  - Existing header/title input patterns from story page.
  - Existing card, button, and focus-outline patterns from Stories/Home surfaces.
- New build:
  - Submission Package document tab component (4 docs).
  - Version history list component with status chip.
  - Metadata refresh confirmation flow specific to template overwrite behavior.
  - Export action group for DOCX bundle + plain-text copy blocks.

## Feasibility notes
- Metadata refresh must be explicit and diff-aware in UX: user confirms before overwriting edited template regions.
- Independent per-document saves require clear per-document dirty/saved indicators to prevent cross-document ambiguity.
- Version history semantics should be package-level snapshots with traceable timestamp/status; avoid hidden autosave-only behavior.
- Export UX should tolerate long-running generation without blocking active editing.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Add the new mode entry point where story modes are controlled today: `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx` and integration in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`.
- Keep page-level structure consistent with current story editor containers in `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` (header band, main canvas, optional side rail behavior).
- Follow layout shell and spacing constraints from `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.module.css` and `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.module.css`.
- Use only existing token primitives from `src/styles/tokens.css` for color, spacing, typography, borders, focus, and elevation.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

## Goal
Add a Submission Package view mode to the story editor where writers can create, edit, version, and export a complete set of submission documents (query letter, one-page synopsis, long synopsis, beta-reader briefing) auto-populated with story metadata.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/submissionPackage.ts` — document types, package version, package model
- `src/lib/submissionPackage.ts` — package CRUD, metadata refresh, export (DOCX-like plain-text bundle + clipboard), localStorage persistence
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/SubmissionPackageView.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/PackageDocumentEditor.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/PackageMetaPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/PackageVersionHistory.tsx`
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/PackageStatusChip.tsx`
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/PackageEmptyState.tsx`
- `src/components/SubmissionPackage/MetadataRefreshDialog.tsx` + `.module.css`

**Modified files:**
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx` — add `'package'` mode
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add `'package'` viewMode branch
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — add package layout styles

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/submissionPackage.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type DocType = 'query-letter' | 'synopsis-one-page' | 'synopsis-long' | 'beta-briefing';
   export type PackageStatus = 'draft' | 'ready' | 'submitted';
   export interface PackageMetadata {
     title: string;
     genre: string;
     wordCount: string;
     logline: string;
     comps: string;           // comparable titles
     protagonistSummary: string;
     conflictSummary: string;
   }
   export interface PackageDocument {
     type: DocType;
     content: string;         // editable rich-text as plain text for v1
     lastEditedAt: number;
   }
   export interface PackageVersion {
     id: string;
     label: string;           // e.g., "v1 · Draft · 2025-03-16"
     status: PackageStatus;
     documents: PackageDocument[];
     metadata: PackageMetadata;
     createdAt: number;
   }
   export interface SubmissionPackage {
     id: string;
     storyId: string;
     status: PackageStatus;
     documents: PackageDocument[];
     metadata: PackageMetadata;
     versions: PackageVersion[];
     createdAt: number;
     updatedAt: number;
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/submissionPackage.ts`**:
   - `PKG_KEY = (storyId: string) => \`story-${storyId}-package\``
   - `getPackage(storyId): SubmissionPackage | null`
   - `createPackage(storyId, metadata: PackageMetadata): SubmissionPackage` — seeds document templates (see below); persists
   - `updateDocument(storyId, docType: DocType, content: string)` — patches document content, updates `updatedAt`
   - `updateStatus(storyId, status: PackageStatus)` — patch package status
   - `refreshMetadata(storyId, newMetadata: PackageMetadata)` — replaces metadata field, refreshes metadata-driven placeholders in document templates (only unfilled placeholder tokens `{{placeholder}}`, not user-edited text)
   - `saveVersion(storyId, label?: string): PackageVersion` — snapshots current documents + metadata + status as a version entry; trims to 20 versions max
   - `restoreVersion(storyId, versionId: string)` — saves current as version first, then replaces documents with version snapshot
   - `exportBundle(pkg: SubmissionPackage): void` — creates a single `Blob` (`text/plain`) with all 4 documents separated by `=== [Doc Name] ===` headers; triggers download as `submission-package.txt`
   - `exportPlainText(pkg: SubmissionPackage, docType: DocType): void` — copies single document to clipboard via `navigator.clipboard.writeText`
   - **Document templates** (pure functions, called by `createPackage`):
     - `queryLetterTemplate(meta): string` — scaffold with placeholder tokens for `{{title}}`, `{{genre}}`, `{{wordCount}}`, `{{logline}}`, `{{comps}}`, `{{protagonist}}`, `{{conflict}}`; fills known tokens from metadata; leaves blank sections for writer
     - `synopsisOnePageTemplate(meta): string` — single-page scaffold (opening hook, core conflict, resolution placeholders)
     - `synopsisLongTemplate(meta): string` — multi-section scaffold (Act 1 / Act 2 / Act 3 structure)
     - `betaBriefingTemplate(meta): string` — instructions scaffold for beta readers: what to look for, genre context, focus questions

3. **Create `PackageDocumentEditor`**:
   - Props: `document: PackageDocument`, `onChange(content: string)`, `onSave()`
   - Renders: document type heading (e.g., "Query Letter"), editable `<textarea>` (serif font matching story editor for prose feel)
   - Auto-save on blur (calls `onSave`); shows "Saved" feedback for 2s after save
   - Scaffold section headings as read-only comments if document is on template default (writer can delete once editing begins)

4. **Create `PackageMetaPanel`** (right rail):
   - Props: `metadata`, `status`, `versions`, `onMetadataChange`, `onRefresh`, `onStatusChange`, `onExportBundle`, `onVersionSave`, `onVersionRestore`
   - Sections:
     - Package status chip (`draft | ready | submitted`) — clickable to cycle; labeled `PackageStatusChip`
     - Metadata card: editable fields (title, genre, word count, logline, comps, protagonist, conflict); "last synced" timestamp
     - "Refresh metadata into templates" button → opens `MetadataRefreshDialog` (confirms that only unfilled placeholders will be updated, not writer-edited content)
     - Version history list (`PackageVersionHistory`): timestamped version entries with status badge; Restore button
     - Export section: "Export bundle (text)" + "Copy query letter" + "Copy synopsis"

5. **Create `PackageVersionHistory`**:
   - List of version rows (newest first): label, status chip, timestamp
   - "Restore" button on each version → calls `onVersionRestore(versionId)` → saves current as new version first, then restores
   - Empty state: "Versions appear after first save"

6. **Create `PackageStatusChip`**:
   - Inline badge with border; label: "Draft" | "Ready" | "Submitted"
   - Cycles on click (draft → ready → submitted → draft)
   - Announce status change to `aria-live` region

7. **Create `MetadataRefreshDialog`**:
   - Modal: "Refresh story metadata into templates? Only unfilled placeholder sections will be updated. Your edits will not be changed."
   - Confirm | Cancel buttons; focus trap; Escape closes

8. **Create `PackageEmptyState`**:
   - 3-step visual guide: "1. Set your story details → 2. Edit the templates → 3. Export your package"
   - Single CTA: "Create submission package" → opens metadata form inline

9. **Create `SubmissionPackageView` container** (`SubmissionPackageView.tsx`):
   - State: `pkg: SubmissionPackage | null`, `activeDoc: DocType` (default `'query-letter'`), `loading`
   - On mount: load from `getPackage(storyId)`
   - First-time empty state: show `PackageEmptyState` until package is created
   - Layout: two-column grid (main editor column + right rail `PackageMetaPanel`)
   - Document tab row: 4 tabs (Query Letter | 1-Page Synopsis | Long Synopsis | Beta Briefing) above the editor column
   - Active tab renders `PackageDocumentEditor` for the selected doc
   - Auto-save on doc change (blur or after 3s debounce)
   - Success feedback: lightweight "Saved" inline text after auto-save, "Version saved" after explicit version save, "Exported" after export

10. **Update `ViewModeSwitch`**:
    - Add `'package'` to `ViewMode` type
    - Add 4th button "Package" in the switch; existing 3 buttons unchanged
    - Update `aria-label` to "Switch to submission package view"

11. **Update `page.tsx`**:
    - Add `viewMode === 'package'` branch in the render
    - Render `<SubmissionPackageView storyId={id} storyTitle={storyTitle} viewMode={viewMode} onViewModeChange={setViewMode} />` in a `.packageLayout` wrapper
    - No changes needed to editor or corkboard branches

12. **Update `page.module.css`**:
    - `.packageLayout` — full height, `overflow: hidden`
    - Package view styles are in `SubmissionPackage.module.css`

13. **Add `SubmissionPackage.module.css`** (inside `src/components/SubmissionPackage/`):
    - `.view` — two-column desktop layout: `grid-template-columns: 1fr 280px`, full height
    - `.mainCol` — editor column with document tabs and editor area
    - `.docTabs` — segmented tab row above editor; reuse `ViewModeSwitch` visual style for consistency
    - `.docEditor` — serif textarea matching story editor style
    - `.rightRail` — right panel, `--color-polar-100` bg, overflow-y auto
    - `.metaSection` — card block in right rail with border and padding
    - `.metaField` — label + input row, compact spacing
    - `.versionRow` — version list item with status chip, timestamp, restore button
    - Responsive: tablet → right rail collapses below editor as stacked cards; mobile → doc tabs become scrollable pills, actions collapse to overflow

## Test plan

- **Create package**: first visit → empty state → click "Create submission package" → metadata form → submit → document editor shows query letter template with filled metadata placeholders
- **Edit documents**: edit query letter text → save (blur) → "Saved" appears; switch to Synopsis tab → edits preserved across tab switches
- **Metadata refresh**: change story title in metadata panel → click "Refresh metadata into templates" → dialog confirms → templates update `{{title}}` placeholders; writer's custom text unaffected
- **Status change**: click status chip → cycles draft → ready → submitted; status badge updates
- **Version save**: click "Save version" → entry appears in version history with timestamp and current status
- **Version restore**: click Restore on a previous version → current saves as new version → editor loads restored content
- **Export bundle**: click "Export bundle" → `.txt` file downloads with all 4 documents separated by section headers
- **Copy document**: click "Copy query letter" → clipboard contains query letter text
- **Persistence**: refresh page → package content, versions, and metadata reload from localStorage
- **Tabs keyboard**: Tab to document tab buttons → arrow keys navigate; Enter activates; editor receives focus after tab switch
- **Modal keyboard**: Refresh dialog — Tab between Confirm/Cancel; Escape cancels
- **View mode switch**: click "Package" in ViewModeSwitch → package view loads; click "Editor" → returns to editor; previously edited content unchanged

## Risks and mitigations

- **Template placeholder refresh**: only replace tokens matching `{{placeholder}}` that still contain the template default text (not any writer-edited content). Use a regex replace only on unedited tokens. Document this clearly in code with a comment.
- **4th ViewMode button width**: "Package" button in ViewModeSwitch may be too long. Abbreviate label to "Package" (6 chars — acceptable). Verify layout does not break on mobile.
- **Export format**: DOCX export requires a library (not in current deps). v1 uses plain-text `.txt` format instead. DOCX bundle is deferred. Note in acceptance criteria.
- **Metadata field verbosity**: keep all metadata fields as plain text inputs in v1; no rich text or character counting needed

Remove `'package'` from `ViewMode` union in `ViewModeSwitch.tsx` and remove the `viewMode === 'package'` branch in `page.tsx`. All package data remains in localStorage but inaccessible; future re-enable is possible without data loss.

- `console.log('SubmissionPackage: version saved', { versionId, storyId })`
- `console.log('SubmissionPackage: exported bundle', { storyId })`

title: Add read-aloud revision mode with pacing, repetition, and dialogue clarity highlights
description:
## Problem
Many narrative issues (awkward cadence, repeated phrasing, unnatural dialogue beats) are hard to catch while silently reading. Writers need a revision pass tuned for voice and flow quality.

## Target user
Drafting-stage novelists and editors polishing prose quality before beta-reader sharing or submission.

## Outcome / KPI
- 25% increase in usage of revision tooling per active manuscript.
- 20% decrease in repeat phrasing flags in final manuscript versions.
- 15% faster time from first draft to “ready for feedback” milestone.

## Scope
- Add a read-aloud mode at chapter/scene level with play/pause and sentence-follow cursor.
- Highlight potential prose issues during playback: repeated phrases, long-run sentences, and dense dialogue blocks lacking beats/tags.
- Provide quick actions from each highlight: jump to sentence, dismiss, or mark for later.
- Add “Revision pass summary” with counts by issue type and completion progress.
- Preserve writer control: all suggestions are optional and reversible.

## Non-goals
- Fully autonomous rewriting of scenes.
- Language translation or multilingual grammar tooling.
- Accessibility-grade screen reader replacement.

## Acceptance criteria
- User can run read-aloud mode on any scene/chapter and see synchronized sentence focus.
- System surfaces at least three issue classes (repetition, long sentence cadence, dialogue clarity) with clickable locations.
- Dismissing an issue removes it from active list without editing text automatically.
- Revision summary updates after edits and supports rerun.
- Feature remains usable without enabling broader AI generation features.

## Priority rationale
Living Writer-style structure helps writers organize stories; this feature improves prose finish quality directly in-app, creating a stronger “draft to publish-ready” workflow and a differentiated quality pass beyond basic outlining.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

[Design]:
## UX goal
Create a focused revision pass that helps writers hear and fix prose flow issues (cadence, repetition, dialogue clarity) without breaking drafting momentum. Read-aloud should feel like a temporary assistive layer on top of the existing editor, not a separate complicated tool.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: manuscript text area with sentence-follow focus during playback.
2. Secondary: compact playback controls (play/pause, speed, sentence progress, rerun scan).
3. Tertiary: issue list grouped by type (Repetition, Long sentence cadence, Dialogue clarity) with per-item actions (Jump, Dismiss, Mark for later).
4. Summary layer: revision pass summary (total issues, by type, resolved/dismissed/remaining) visible in the right panel.

## Layout and components
- Keep current editor-first layout in story view: main editor + right sidebar.
- In editor mode header, place a one-click "Read Aloud" toggle near existing view controls; playback controls appear inline when active.
- Use lightweight sentence highlight in the editor while audio plays (single active sentence + subtle issue underlines for flagged spans).
- Convert current sidebar placeholder into a "Revision" panel with:
  - summary card at top
  - issue-type filter tabs/chips
  - scrollable issue list with jump/dismiss/mark actions
- Keep corkboard view unchanged for v1 except optional entry affordance to open revision mode from scene context.

## Interaction states
- Idle: revision panel collapsed to a clear CTA ("Run read-aloud pass").
- Loading: show skeleton rows in revision panel and disabled playback controls with "Analyzing scene…" status text.
- Empty: explicit copy when no issues are found ("Great flow — no cadence/repetition/dialogue flags detected") plus rerun action.
- Error: non-blocking inline alert in panel (analysis/playback failed) with retry; editor remains fully usable.
- Success: issue list and summary update immediately after dismiss/mark, with lightweight confirmation text and undo for recent dismiss.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: all playback and issue actions must be reachable via tab order and activatable by Enter/Space.
- Focus: when "Jump" is selected, move focus to editor and visibly focus the target sentence; preserve return path to issue list.
- Contrast: use existing grayscale tokens with at least readable contrast for text and focus rings; do not rely on color alone for issue type distinction (add labels/icons/text badges).
- Text clarity: concise, writer-friendly labels (avoid NLP jargon); sentence-length warnings should explain "why flagged" in plain language.

## Visual style
- Match current minimalist grayscale UI and border language from tokens in `src/styles/tokens.css`.
- Surfaces: `--color-white` for primary editor cards, `--color-polar-100` for panel/background sections.
- Dividers/borders: `--color-fashion-300`; interactive emphasis/focus: `--color-carbon-600`; primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary copy: `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Keep prose-first typography in editor body (existing serif writing area), with sans-serif utility text in controls/panel for scannability.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024px): keep two-column editor + revision panel with sticky playback controls at top of editor region.
- Tablet (768-1023px): hide persistent sidebar by default; revision panel opens as a slide-over panel to protect writing width.
- Mobile (<768px): revision UI becomes a bottom sheet/full-height panel; sentence jump returns to editor and closes sheet unless user pins it open.

## Codebase alignment
- Reviewed current shell and editor structure in:
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`
  - `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`
  - `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`
  - `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`
  - `src/styles/tokens.css`
- Brief is aligned to existing architecture: editor mode already has header, canvas, and sidebar zones that can host revision controls/panel without a route redesign.

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:
  - existing story editor route/layout and title input pattern
  - existing focus styles, spacing, and grayscale tokens
  - existing toolbar interaction conventions from view switch and corkboard toolbars
- New build:
  - read-aloud control cluster
  - sentence-sync highlight treatment in editor content
  - revision issue list + summary module in sidebar
  - issue-type badges/filter controls specific to revision pass

## Feasibility notes
- Current editor is a plain `textarea`; precise sentence-level highlight and in-text flagged spans are not feasible inside native textarea rendering.
- For true sentence-follow + inline issue highlighting, a structural editor surface change is likely required (contenteditable/rich-text layer or split render overlay).
- If keeping textarea for v1, fallback UX should prioritize panel-driven navigation (show sentence excerpt + caret jump) instead of inline decorated text.
- Dismiss/mark actions must be reversible in-session to preserve writer trust and avoid accidental loss of review context.

- Anchor new revision controls in the existing `.editorHeader` region of `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`.
- Anchor revision panel in existing `.sidebar`/`.sidebarSection` slots of `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and styles in `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`.

- Preserve current keyboard and skip-link behavior from `AppShell` while introducing new interactive controls.
- Modes should remain one-click to enter/exit; no nested setup flow before playback.

<plan>

## Goal
Add a Read-Aloud revision mode in the story editor that plays back the manuscript with sentence-level focus tracking and surfaces three classes of prose issues (repetition, long sentences, dialogue clarity) with dismiss/mark-for-later actions.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/readAloud.ts` — issue type definitions
- `src/lib/readAloud.ts` — sentence splitting, issue detection (repetition, long sentence, dialogue clarity)
- `src/hooks/useReadAloud.ts` — Web Speech API playback, sentence tracking, issue state management
- `src/components/ReadAloudPanel/ReadAloudPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/ReadAloudPanel/ReadAloudControls.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/ReadAloudPanel/IssueList.tsx`
- `src/components/ReadAloudPanel/IssueRow.tsx`
- `src/components/ReadAloudPanel/RevisionSummaryCard.tsx`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add Read Aloud toggle, sidebar mode for Read Aloud, textarea ref
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — extend sidebar styles for Read Aloud panel

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/readAloud.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type IssueType = 'repetition' | 'long-sentence' | 'dialogue-clarity';
   export type IssueStatus = 'open' | 'dismissed' | 'marked';
   export interface RevisionIssue {
     id: string;
     type: IssueType;
     sentenceIndex: number;
     sentenceText: string;
     description: string; // plain-language explanation
     status: IssueStatus;
   }
   export interface Sentence {
     index: number;
     text: string;
     startChar: number; // character offset in full content
     endChar: number;
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/readAloud.ts`**:
   - `splitSentences(content: string): Sentence[]` — split on sentence-ending punctuation (`.!?` followed by space, newline, or end of string), tracking `startChar`/`endChar` offsets
   - `detectRepetition(sentences: Sentence[]): RevisionIssue[]` — sliding window of 5 sentences; flag sentences where a 3+ word phrase appears in any prior sentence within the window. Description: "Repeated phrase detected nearby"
   - `detectLongSentences(sentences: Sentence[]): RevisionIssue[]` — flag sentences > 35 words. Description: "Long sentence may slow pacing ({n} words)"
   - `detectDialogueClarity(sentences: Sentence[]): RevisionIssue[]` — flag runs of ≥3 consecutive sentences containing quotation marks but no dialogue tag words (`said|asked|replied|whispered|shouted`). Description: "Consecutive dialogue without beats or tags ({n} lines)"
   - `analyzeContent(content: string): { sentences: Sentence[]; issues: RevisionIssue[] }` — calls all three detectors, deduplicates by sentence index, assigns unique IDs

3. **Create `src/hooks/useReadAloud.ts`**:
   - Props: `content: string`, `textareaRef: React.RefObject<HTMLTextAreaElement>`
   - State: `isActive`, `isPlaying`, `isAnalyzing`, `sentences: Sentence[]`, `issues: RevisionIssue[]`, `currentSentenceIndex: number`
   - `activate()`:
     - Set `isAnalyzing = true`
     - In next tick (or `setTimeout(0)`): call `analyzeContent(content)` → set sentences/issues, set `isAnalyzing = false`, set `isActive = true`
   - `deactivate()` → stop any active speech, reset all state to defaults
   - `togglePlayback()`:
     - If not playing: create `SpeechSynthesisUtterance` for `sentences[currentSentenceIndex].text`, set `onend` to advance to next sentence and play recursively, call `window.speechSynthesis.speak(utterance)`; set `isPlaying = true`
     - If playing: `window.speechSynthesis.pause()`, set `isPlaying = false`
   - `jumpToSentence(sentenceIndex: number)`:
     - Set `currentSentenceIndex = sentenceIndex`
     - Call `textareaRef.current?.setSelectionRange(sentences[sentenceIndex].startChar, sentences[sentenceIndex].endChar)`
     - Call `textareaRef.current?.focus()`
   - `dismissIssue(id)` → update issue status to `'dismissed'`
   - `markIssue(id)` → update issue status to `'marked'`
   - `rerunAnalysis()` → re-analyzes with current content (useful after edits)
   - Check `typeof window !== 'undefined' && 'speechSynthesis' in window` before using speech API; if unavailable, `isPlaybackSupported = false`

4. **Create `ReadAloudControls` component**:
   - Props: `isActive`, `isPlaying`, `isAnalyzing`, `isPlaybackSupported`, `currentSentenceIndex`, `totalSentences`, `onToggle` (activate/deactivate), `onPlayPause`, `onRerun`
   - Idle: "Read Aloud" toggle button in editor header (outside panel)
   - Active controls (compact bar at top of panel or editorHeader when active): play/pause button, sentence counter ("Sentence 3 of 42"), speed selector (0.75x / 1x / 1.25x), rerun button
   - If `!isPlaybackSupported`: show "Playback not supported in this browser. Issue highlights still available."

5. **Create `IssueList` and `IssueRow`**:
   - `IssueList`: filter tabs (All | Repetition | Long Sentence | Dialogue) using `aria-selected` tab pattern; scrollable list of `IssueRow`
   - `IssueRow`: type badge (text label), sentence excerpt (truncated to 80 chars), description, action buttons: Jump (focuses textarea at sentence), Dismiss (removes from active list), Mark for later (adds ★ marker)
   - Dismissed issues hidden from main list; marked issues shown with ★ indicator
   - All action buttons: `type="button"`, `aria-label`, keyboard accessible

6. **Create `RevisionSummaryCard`**:
   - Shows: total issues detected, breakdown by type (e.g., "3 repetitions · 2 long sentences · 1 dialogue flag"), resolved/dismissed/remaining counts
   - "Rerun pass" button to re-analyze after edits
   - Updates live as issues are dismissed/marked

7. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Add `textareaRef = useRef<HTMLTextAreaElement>(null)` and attach to the `<textarea>`
   - Import and call `useReadAloud({ content, textareaRef })` → read aloud state/actions
   - Add `sidebarMode: 'notes' | 'history' | 'readAloud'` state (expand existing sidebar tab pattern from BIN-93 if already implemented, or add new tab bar now)
   - In `.editorHeader`: add `ReadAloudControls` toggle button (renders inline controls when active)
   - When `sidebarMode === 'readAloud'`, render `ReadAloudPanel` (contains `IssueList` + `RevisionSummaryCard`) in sidebar
   - Activating Read Aloud auto-switches `sidebarMode` to `'readAloud'`
   - Deactivating restores `sidebarMode` to previous value

8. **Create `ReadAloudPanel` wrapper** (`ReadAloudPanel.tsx`):
   - Container for `RevisionSummaryCard` + `IssueList`
   - Handles loading skeleton (3 skeleton rows + summary skeleton) when `isAnalyzing`
   - Empty state: "Great flow — no cadence/repetition/dialogue flags detected" + rerun button
   - Error state (if analysis throws): inline alert with retry

9. **Update `page.module.css`**:
   - Extend sidebar tab bar (add "Read Aloud" tab if sidebar tabs not yet added)
   - `.readAloudPanel` — scrollable flex column in sidebar, padding tokens
   - `.issueRow` — compact card: border, `--color-white` bg, hover `--color-fog-200`
   - `.typeBadge` — small inline text badge, bordered, sans-serif
   - `.summaryCard` — top of panel, light `--color-polar-100` bg, summary stats row
   - All responsive: mobile becomes full-height slide-over (add `data-read-aloud-mobile-panel` toggle)
   - Tablet: panel opens as slide-over; editor column retains full width (set `position: fixed` or `absolute` for overlay)

## Test plan

- **Activate**: click "Read Aloud" → panel opens, analysis runs, issues appear grouped by type
- **Playback**: click Play → speech starts on sentence 1; sentence text highlighted in panel list; advances to sentence 2 on completion
- **Pause/resume**: Pause → speech pauses; Play → resumes from same sentence
- **Speed**: change speed to 1.25x → speech rate changes (`utterance.rate`)
- **Jump**: click Jump on issue row → textarea focuses, selection moves to that sentence's char range
- **Dismiss**: click Dismiss → issue removed from active list; summary count decrements
- **Mark for later**: click Mark → ★ indicator added, issue moves to marked section or stays with badge
- **Summary**: counts update correctly as issues are resolved
- **Rerun**: after editing content, click Rerun → re-analyses with new content, issues list updates
- **Empty state**: content with no detectable issues → "Great flow" empty message
- **No speech API**: force `window.speechSynthesis = undefined` → issue detection still works, playback controls show graceful fallback message
- **Keyboard**: Tab through all panel controls; Enter/Space on Jump/Dismiss/Mark; filter tabs reachable via keyboard

## Risks and mitigations

- **Sentence splitting edge cases**: abbreviations (`Mr.`, `e.g.`) may cause false sentence splits. For v1, this is acceptable; note in code for future improvement. Writers can use Rerun after editing.
- **Speech synthesis browser support**: check for `window.speechSynthesis` guard. On unsupported browsers, issue detection panel still works.
- **Performance on long manuscripts**: `analyzeContent` runs in a `setTimeout(0)` to avoid blocking the main thread. For very long manuscripts (>50k words), may still be slow. Add `isAnalyzing` skeleton UX.
- **Textarea caret jump on mobile**: `setSelectionRange` may scroll on mobile in unexpected ways. Test on iOS Safari; if problematic, show sentence excerpt in panel only (no caret jump) for mobile.

## Rollback strategy
All code is additive. Remove the "Read Aloud" toggle button from `.editorHeader` in `page.tsx` to disable entry to the feature. Existing editor remains unaffected.

## Observability
- `console.log('ReadAloud: analysis complete', { sentenceCount, issueCount })` after analysis
- `console.log('ReadAloud: issue dismissed/marked', { issueId, type })` on user actions

## Acceptance mapping
- "User can run read-aloud mode on any scene/chapter and see synchronized sentence focus" → `useReadAloud` advances `currentSentenceIndex` via `utterance.onend`, panel highlights current sentence
- "System surfaces at least three issue classes (repetition, long sentence cadence, dialogue clarity)" → `detectRepetition`, `detectLongSentences`, `detectDialogueClarity` in `readAloud.ts`
- "Dismissing an issue removes it from active list without editing text automatically" → `dismissIssue()` sets status, `IssueList` filters dismissed items
- "Revision summary updates after edits and supports rerun" → `RevisionSummaryCard` + `rerunAnalysis()`
- "Feature remains usable without enabling broader AI generation features" → entirely local static analysis, no API calls

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>

title: Ship scene version history with diff preview and safe restore
description:
## Problem
Writers need confidence that drafts are never lost and that risky revisions can be undone later. Current flows emphasize drafting speed, but there is no explicit manuscript history experience for restoring earlier versions.

## Target user
Writers iterating through multiple revision passes who want to experiment safely without copying text into external backups.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce incidents of users reporting lost or overwritten content
- Increase usage of revision workflows (edits per session, return edits after pause)
- Increase retention among users with multi-session projects

## Scope
- Add automatic scene snapshots at meaningful checkpoints (manual save, significant text delta, and pre-revision-pass actions).
- Add a Version History panel for each scene with timestamped entries and optional short labels.
- Provide side-by-side diff preview (current vs selected snapshot) before restore.
- Allow one-click restore of a snapshot into the active scene with confirmation.
- Keep a short audit trail of restore actions in history.

## Non-goals
- Full Git-like branching/merge model
- Cross-story compare views
- Collaborative multi-author conflict resolution in this ticket

## Acceptance criteria
- Scene history list shows timestamped snapshots in reverse chronological order.
- Writer can open any snapshot and preview text differences before restoring.
- Restore action requires explicit confirmation and creates a new latest snapshot (so restores are reversible).
- Snapshot creation and restore do not noticeably block editor typing performance.
- History remains available after refresh/reopen of the same story.

## Priority rationale
Trust is foundational for long-form writing products. A robust history flow reduces fear of revision and strengthens differentiation versus lightweight editors that do not protect iterative drafting deeply.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

## Competitor evidence

Proceed, with design emphasis on calm trust cues: simple timeline labels, low-friction compare flow, explicit restore confirmation, and an immediate post-restore rollback path. ## UX goal
Give writers a calm, trust-first “Version History” flow inside the scene editor so they can experiment aggressively, compare revisions quickly, and restore safely without fear of data loss.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: current scene writing surface remains dominant.
2. Secondary: version timeline and selected snapshot metadata in a right-side panel (desktop) / slide-over (mobile).
3. Tertiary: diff preview and restore controls, shown only when a snapshot is selected.
4. Audit context: recent restore events visible in timeline as distinct entries.

## Layout and components
- Keep the existing editor shell in [src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx] and [src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css] with a 2-column desktop editor layout.
- Add a new “History” section in the existing editor sidebar area (`.sidebar`) so Notes and History can be stacked sections (or tabbed if vertical space is constrained).
- History section structure:
  - Header row: “Version History” + lightweight status text (e.g., “Auto-saved just now”).
  - Reverse-chronological list of snapshot rows (timestamp, optional label, restore badge when applicable).
  - Snapshot row selection opens diff preview region below list.
- Diff preview:
  - Side-by-side panes: “Current draft” and “Selected version”.
  - Preserve readable prose width and relaxed line-height; avoid dense code-like diff visuals.
  - Inline emphasis for changed blocks only (minimal highlighting, not every character).
- Restore action:
  - Primary button in diff footer: “Restore this version”.
  - Confirmation dialog with consequence copy and explicit CTA.
  - Post-restore success banner in sidebar: “Version restored. A new snapshot was created.”

## Interaction states
- Idle: list shows latest snapshots; newest entry appears as active baseline.
- Loading: skeleton rows in history list and muted placeholders in diff panes.
- Empty: concise message (“No snapshots yet. Snapshots appear automatically as you write.”).
- Error: inline error block in panel with retry action; editor remains fully usable.
- Success: non-blocking confirmation after restore; timeline prepends a new restore snapshot and labels it.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard:
  - Snapshot rows are tabbable buttons; Enter/Space selects snapshot.

- Contrast:
  - Use approved grayscale tokens with at least AA contrast for body text and controls.
- Text clarity:
  - Writer-facing labels should be plain language (“Current draft”, “Saved 2:14 PM”, “Restored”).

## Visual style
- Match existing grayscale system in [src/styles/tokens.css]:
  - Background surfaces: `--color-white`, `--color-polar-100`

- Maintain current minimalist, low-noise style and serif writing canvas in editor.
- Avoid adding accent colors for this feature; trust should come from hierarchy and copy clarity, not chroma.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024px): persistent sidebar history panel alongside editor.
- Tablet (768-1023px): keep editor-first layout; history can be a collapsible panel below header or a narrow slide-over to avoid squeezing text area.
- Mobile (<768px): history opens from a one-tap control in editor header as a full-height sheet; diff uses stacked panes (Current first, Selected second).

- Reviewed and aligned with current app structure:

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse:

- New build:
  - Scene history panel component(s) (timeline list, snapshot item, diff preview, restore confirmation dialog, restore success banner).

## Feasibility notes
- Snapshot list virtualization may be needed if histories grow large; otherwise scrolling in sidebar should suffice for v1.
- Diff rendering should avoid full-document reflow while typing; compute/refresh on snapshot selection, not every keystroke.
- Restore must always append a new latest snapshot to keep restores reversible and preserve audit trail.

## Goal
Add automatic story content snapshots and a Version History panel in the editor sidebar with diff preview and safe, reversible restore.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/storyHistory.ts` — snapshot type definitions
- `src/lib/storyHistory.ts` — snapshot CRUD, diff computation, localStorage persistence
- `src/hooks/useStoryHistory.ts` — auto-snapshot logic, manual snapshot, restore
- `src/components/StoryHistory/StoryHistoryPanel.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/StoryHistory/SnapshotRow.tsx`
- `src/components/StoryHistory/DiffPreview.tsx` + `.module.css`
- `src/components/StoryHistory/RestoreDialog.tsx` + `.module.css`

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` — add history hook, sidebar tab state, History section
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` — extend sidebar layout, add history panel styles

## Step-by-step plan

1. **Define types** in `src/types/storyHistory.ts`:
   ```ts
   export type SnapshotTrigger = 'auto' | 'manual' | 'pre-revision' | 'restore';
   export interface StorySnapshot {
     id: string;
     storyId: string;
     content: string;
     label?: string;
     trigger: SnapshotTrigger;
     createdAt: number; // ms timestamp
   }
   ```

2. **Create `src/lib/storyHistory.ts`**:
   - `HISTORY_KEY = (storyId: string) => \`story-${storyId}-history\``
   - `MAX_SNAPSHOTS = 50`
   - `getSnapshots(storyId)` → `StorySnapshot[]` sorted newest-first; returns `[]` if empty
   - `createSnapshot(storyId, content, trigger, label?)` → saves new snapshot, trims to MAX_SNAPSHOTS, returns created snapshot
   - `deleteSnapshot(storyId, snapshotId)` → removes by id
   - `computeDiff(current: string, older: string): DiffLine[]` where `DiffLine = { type: 'added' | 'removed' | 'unchanged'; text: string }` — simple line-by-line LCS comparison (no external library)
   - `formatSnapshotLabel(s: StorySnapshot)` → human-readable timestamp + trigger text

3. **Create `src/hooks/useStoryHistory.ts`**:
   - Props: `storyId: string`, `content: string`
   - State: `snapshots: StorySnapshot[]`, loaded lazily from `getSnapshots`
   - Auto-snapshot logic: use `useRef` to track `lastSnapshotContent` and `lastSnapshotTime`. Fire auto-snapshot (trigger `'auto'`) when:
     - Content delta from last snap >= 300 characters, OR
     - 5 minutes elapsed since last auto-snap AND content has changed
   - Never auto-snapshot during rapid typing (debounce 10s before committing auto-snap)
   - `manualSnapshot(label?)` — calls `createSnapshot` with trigger `'manual'`, refreshes snapshots state
   - `restoreSnapshot(snapshotId)` — first creates a `'restore'` snapshot of current content, then returns the selected snapshot content (caller applies to content state); refreshes snapshot list
   - `refreshSnapshots()` — reloads from localStorage

4. **Create `StoryHistoryPanel`** (`src/components/StoryHistory/StoryHistoryPanel.tsx`):
   - Props: `snapshots`, `currentContent`, `onRestore(content: string)`, `onManualSnapshot(label?: string)`
   - Contains header "Version History" + status text ("Auto-saved just now" relative to latest snap)
   - Renders scrollable list of `SnapshotRow` items (newest first)
   - When a snapshot row is selected, renders `DiffPreview` below the list
   - `RestoreDialog` shown when user confirms restore action
   - Empty state: "No snapshots yet. Snapshots appear automatically as you write."
   - Loading/skeleton: show 3 skeleton rows on initial load

5. **Create `SnapshotRow`** (`src/components/StoryHistory/SnapshotRow.tsx`):
   - Displays: formatted timestamp, optional label, trigger badge (Auto / Manual / Restore)
   - On click: selects this snapshot (highlights row)
   - Keyboard: `role="button"`, Enter/Space triggers select
   - Restore badge for snapshots with `trigger === 'restore'`

6. **Create `DiffPreview`** (`src/components/StoryHistory/DiffPreview.tsx`):
   - Props: `current: string`, `older: string`
   - Desktop: two side-by-side panes ("Current draft" | "Selected version") with scrollable prose text
   - Mobile: stacked panes (current first, older second)
   - Renders plain prose with changed lines lightly emphasized (e.g., `background: var(--color-fog-200)`)
   - Max height: `320px` with overflow-y scroll
   - Footer: "Restore this version" primary button that triggers `RestoreDialog`

7. **Create `RestoreDialog`** (`src/components/StoryHistory/RestoreDialog.tsx`):
   - Modal overlay (`var(--z-modal)`) with focus trap
   - Copy: "Restore this version? Your current draft will be saved as a new snapshot first."
   - Primary CTA: "Restore" | Secondary: "Cancel" (Escape closes)
   - Post-restore: success banner in sidebar ("Version restored. A new snapshot was created."), focus moves to success message

8. **Update `page.tsx`**:
   - Import `useStoryHistory` and `StoryHistoryPanel`
   - Call `useStoryHistory(id, content)` to get `{ snapshots, manualSnapshot, restoreSnapshot }`
   - Add `sidebarMode: 'notes' | 'history'` state (default `'notes'`)
   - In the sidebar `<aside>`, render a 2-tab row ("Notes" | "History") above the sections
   - When `sidebarMode === 'history'` render `StoryHistoryPanel`; otherwise render existing Notes placeholder
   - On restore: call `restoreSnapshot(snapshotId)` → get content back → call `setContent(restoredContent)`
   - Create a snapshot before restore via the hook's built-in pre-restore logic

9. **Update `page.module.css`**:
   - `.sidebarTabBar` — horizontal flex row for Notes/History tabs, border-bottom separator
   - `.sidebarTab` / `.sidebarTabActive` — consistent with existing segmented control style; use `--color-black-700` for active, `--color-cement-400` for inactive
   - `.historyList` — scrollable flex column, max-height calculated from viewport
   - `.snapshotRow` / `.snapshotRowSelected` — padding, border, cursor, hover state with `--color-fog-200`
   - `.triggerBadge` — small inline badge (border + label, no color fill)
   - `.diffPanes` / `.diffPane` — side-by-side at >=768px, stacked below
   - `.diffLine` / `.diffLineAdded` / `.diffLineRemoved` — subtle background for changed lines
   - `.restoreDialogOverlay` / `.restoreDialogCard` — modal presentation
   - All styles use only token variables from `src/styles/tokens.css`

## Test plan

- **Auto-snapshot**: type 300+ chars → check `localStorage` key `story-{id}-history` for new entry; verify trigger is `'auto'`
- **Manual snapshot**: invoke `manualSnapshot('before revision')` → verify snapshot appears at top of list with label
- **Snapshot list**: switch sidebar to "History" tab → snapshots display newest-first with timestamps
- **Diff preview**: click a snapshot → diff panes appear with current vs selected; changed lines visible
- **Restore flow**: click "Restore this version" → `RestoreDialog` opens → confirm → editor content changes → new snapshot created with trigger `'restore'`; original still in list (reversible)
- **Restore reversibility**: after restoring, immediately restore again from the pre-restore snapshot → content returns to original
- **Persistence**: reload page → history list repopulates from localStorage
- **Empty state**: new story with no writes → "No snapshots yet" message shown
- **Keyboard**: Tab to snapshot row → Enter selects; Tab to Restore button → Enter opens dialog; Escape closes dialog
- **Performance**: snapshot creation must not block textarea typing; history writes are async post-debounce

## Risks and mitigations

- **Storage quota**: 50-snapshot limit + trimming mitigates localStorage overflow for long stories
- **Diff noise on large prose**: use line-level diff (not word-level) to keep diffs readable; cap diff preview at 320px
- **Concurrent sidebar tabs**: sidebar tab state is local to page; no cross-feature conflict in v1
- **Auto-snap frequency**: 10s debounce + 300-char delta threshold prevents over-snapping during rapid typing

## Rollback strategy
All new code is additive. Removing `useStoryHistory` call from `page.tsx` and the History tab from the sidebar fully disables the feature without breaking existing editor functionality.

## Observability
- `console.log('StoryHistory: snapshot created', { id, trigger, storyId })` on each snapshot save
- `console.log('StoryHistory: restored snapshot', { snapshotId, storyId })` on each restore

## Acceptance mapping
- "Scene history list shows timestamped snapshots in reverse chronological order" → `StoryHistoryPanel` with newest-first `SnapshotRow` list
- "Writer can open any snapshot and preview text differences before restoring" → `DiffPreview` on row select
- "Restore action requires explicit confirmation and creates a new latest snapshot" → `RestoreDialog` + pre-restore snapshot in hook
- "Snapshot creation and restore do not noticeably block editor typing performance" → debounced auto-snap, async writes
- "History remains available after refresh/reopen" → localStorage-backed `getSnapshots`

## Handoff

Assign to: Code Monkey

title: Ship a chapter-aware manuscript editor with scene folding and rich-text controls
description: ## Problem The current drafting surface is not yet a true manuscript editor for long-form fiction. Writers need chapter-aware formatting, clear scene boundaries, and low-friction navigation in one continuous writing flow; otherwise they split work across external tools and lose momentum. ## Target user Solo fiction writers drafting full manuscripts (novels/novellas) who write in long sessions and need structure without leaving the editor. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase average drafting session length per active writer - Increase scenes completed per week - Reduce drop-off between first draft session and week-2 retention ## Scope - Upgrade the writing surface to a chapter-aware manuscript editor with: - basic rich-text controls suitable for fiction drafting (headings, emphasis, inline comments) - visible scene separators and quick scene jump controls - optional scene fold/collapse to focus on the active section - Keep chapter/scene data model compatible with existing navigator and corkboard tickets - Preserve autosave compatibility and recovery behavior ## Non-goals - Full collaborative editing or track-changes workflows - Advanced desktop publishing layout controls - AI writing generation features ## Acceptance criteria - Writer can draft continuously across multiple scenes in one manuscript view - Writer can format chapter/scene text with the supported control set - Writer can collapse non-active scenes and jump between scenes without losing cursor context - Existing autosave/recovery behavior still works in the upgraded editor ## Priority rationale This is core product value and the primary Living Writer-inspired baseline. Several approved tickets depend on a stronger writing surface to deliver meaningful value (feedback, revision, and organization features are weaker without it). ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research --- Recovered from cancelled issue due to checkout 409. title: Ship draft milestone planner with chapter-level progress and finish-date forecast
description: ## Problem Many writers start projects but fail to finish because progress is not translated into a concrete manuscript completion path. Existing writing views show content, not a clear trajectory to “The End.” ## Target user Writers in active first-draft phase who need lightweight accountability and visible momentum. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase percentage of active stories reaching “draft complete” status - Improve week-over-week writing consistency for users who enable milestones - Reduce abandonment rate after first 2 weeks of use ## Scope - Add optional draft milestone setup: target draft length, target completion date, weekly writing cadence - Compute chapter-level progress against draft plan and show remaining effort - Surface a forecast indicator (on-track / at-risk) based on recent writing velocity - Add dashboard entry in story workspace with actionable next milestone - Allow users to pause, edit, or disable milestones without affecting manuscript data ## Non-goals - Public accountability/community leaderboards - Punitive streak mechanics or gamification-heavy UX - AI-generated writing content to meet goals ## Acceptance criteria - User can configure a milestone plan in under 2 minutes - System shows current progress, next milestone, and forecast state for active story - Forecast updates after writing sessions without manual refresh - User can edit or disable milestone plan at any time - Milestone data persists per story across reloads ## Priority rationale Onboarding gets users started, but completion mechanics keep them through the full manuscript lifecycle. Shipping this now strengthens retention and aligns with user need to finish stories, not just organize them. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Ship a draft milestone planner inside the story workspace that lets a writer set a target word count, a target finish date, and a weekly writing cadence. The planner computes chapter-level progress from the existing scenes array and the stored manuscript content, derives a words-per-day velocity, and surfaces a forecast badge (On track / At risk) that updates automatically on every autosave cycle — all without leaving the story workspace and without any modal dialogs. --- ## Scope and files ### New files (8) | Path | Purpose | |---|---| | `src/types/milestone.ts` | `MilestonePlan`, `MilestoneState`, `ChapterProgress`, `ForecastStatus` types | | `src/lib/milestone.ts` | localStorage read/write, word count helpers, forecast computation, chapter grouping | | `src/hooks/useMilestonePlanner.ts` | React hook: loads plan, exposes derived state, handles save/pause/disable | | `src/components/MilestonePlanner/MilestonePlanner.tsx` | Root component: conditionally renders setup form or dashboard | | `src/components/MilestonePlanner/MilestonePlannerSetup.tsx` | Inline form for first-time setup and editing | | `src/components/MilestonePlanner/MilestonePlannerDashboard.tsx` | Dashboard: forecast badge, summary stats, chapter progress list, controls row | | `src/components/MilestonePlanner/ChapterProgressList.tsx` | Renders per-chapter done/drafting/planned counts with word-count proxy | | `src/components/MilestonePlanner/MilestonePlanner.module.css` | All styles for the four components above | ### Modified files (2) | Path | Change | |---|---| | `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` | Import `MilestonePlanner`; render it in the editor sidebar section and in the corkboard layout below the `` | | `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css` | Add `.milestonePlannerSection` (corkboard placement) and mobile responsive rules | --- ## Step-by-step plan ### Step 1 — Define types in `src/types/milestone.ts` ```ts export type ForecastStatus = 'on_track' | 'at_risk' | 'paused' | 'no_plan'; export interface MilestonePlan { /** ISO date string: when the user created/last edited this plan */ createdAt: number; /** Target total word count for the completed draft */ targetWordCount: number; /** Target finish date as unix timestamp (midnight local) */ targetDate: number; /** User's self-reported writing cadence in words per week */ weeklyTarget: number; /** Whether the plan is paused (freeze velocity comparison) */ paused: boolean; /** * Word count at plan creation time — used as baseline so velocity * measures only words written since the plan was started. */ baselineWordCount: number; /** Timestamp when the user paused (to skip paused time from forecast) */ pausedAt: number | null; /** Accumulated paused milliseconds (for correct elapsed-time math) */ pausedDurationMs: number; } export interface ChapterProgress { /** The `chapter` string from Scene, e.g. "Chapter 1" */ chapter: string; plannedCount: number; draftingCount: number; doneCount: number; totalCount: number; /** Proxy word count: sum of words in all scene summaries in this chapter */ summaryWordCount: number; } export interface MilestoneState { plan: MilestonePlan | null; currentWordCount: number; wordsWritten: number; // currentWordCount - baselineWordCount wordsRemaining: number; // targetWordCount - currentWordCount progressPct: number; // 0–100 forecastStatus: ForecastStatus; dailyTargetWords: number; // weeklyTarget / 7 daysRemaining: number; weeksRemaining: number; requiredDailyPace: number; // wordsRemaining / daysRemaining actualDailyPace: number; // wordsWritten / activeElapsedDays chapterProgress: ChapterProgress[]; nextChapterName: string | null; // first chapter with any non-done scenes isLoading: boolean; error: string | null; } ``` --- ### Step 2 — Build the lib layer in `src/lib/milestone.ts` #### 2a. localStorage key ```ts function milestoneKey(storyId: string): string { return `story-${storyId}-milestone`; } ``` This follows the exact key convention already used by `scenes.ts` (`story-${storyId}-scenes`). #### 2b. Load / save / clear ```ts export function loadMilestonePlan(storyId: string): MilestonePlan | null export function saveMilestonePlan(storyId: string, plan: MilestonePlan): void export function clearMilestonePlan(storyId: string): void ``` All three wrap `localStorage` calls in `try/catch` and guard with `typeof window === 'undefined'`, matching the pattern in `autosave.ts`. #### 2c. Word count helper ```ts export function countWords(text: string): number { if (!text.trim()) return 0; return text.trim().split(/\s+/).length; } ``` Used both for the manuscript content (via `loadContent(storyId)`) and for scene summaries. #### 2d. Chapter progress computation ```ts export function computeChapterProgress(scenes: Scene[]): ChapterProgress[] ``` - Group scenes by `scene.chapter` preserving insertion order (use a `Map`). - For each group, count `planned`, `drafting`, `done`. - Compute `summaryWordCount` as the sum of `countWords(scene.summary)` across all scenes in the chapter. - Return the array sorted by the minimum `order` value of scenes in each chapter. #### 2e. Forecast computation ```ts export function computeForecast( plan: MilestonePlan, currentWordCount: number ): Pick ``` Algorithm (all values are integers, floored): ``` wordsWritten = max(0, currentWordCount - plan.baselineWordCount) wordsRemaining = max(0, plan.targetWordCount - currentWordCount) progressPct = floor((currentWordCount / plan.targetWordCount) * 100), clamped 0–100 now = Date.now() activeElapsedMs = (now - plan.createdAt) - plan.pausedDurationMs activeElapsedDays = max(1, activeElapsedMs / 86_400_000) ← floor, min 1 to avoid /0 actualDailyPace = floor(wordsWritten / activeElapsedDays) daysRemaining = max(0, floor((plan.targetDate - now) / 86_400_000)) weeksRemaining = max(0, floor(daysRemaining / 7)) requiredDailyPace = daysRemaining > 0 ? ceil(wordsRemaining / daysRemaining) : wordsRemaining dailyTargetWords = floor(plan.weeklyTarget / 7) forecastStatus: if plan.paused → 'paused' if wordsRemaining <= 0 → 'on_track' (already done) if daysRemaining <= 0 → 'at_risk' (past deadline with words left) if actualDailyPace >= requiredDailyPace → 'on_track' else → 'at_risk' ``` This is a pure function with no side effects — easy to unit test. --- ### Step 3 — Build the hook `src/hooks/useMilestonePlanner.ts` ```ts 'use client'; export interface UseMilestonePlannerResult { state: MilestoneState; savePlan: (draft: Omit) => void; editPlan: (updates: Partial>) => void; pausePlan: () => void; resumePlan: () => void; disablePlan: () => void; } export function useMilestonePlanner(storyId: string, scenes: Scene[]): UseMilestonePlannerResult ``` Internal logic: 1. On mount, call `loadMilestonePlan(storyId)` to set initial `plan` state. 2. Compute `currentWordCount` by calling `countWords(loadContent(storyId) ?? '')` — call this on mount and expose a `refresh()` internal that the hook calls when `saveContent` fires. Since the hook lives in the same component that already receives `content` via `useAutosave`, accept `content: string` as a second argument to avoid re-reading localStorage. This avoids coupling to autosave internals. Updated signature: ```ts export function useMilestonePlanner( storyId: string, scenes: Scene[], content: string // ← pass from useAutosave's content value ): UseMilestonePlannerResult ``` 3. Derive `MilestoneState` via `useMemo` whenever `plan`, `content`, or `scenes` changes. Call `computeForecast` and `computeChapterProgress` inside the memo. 4. `savePlan`: builds a full `MilestonePlan` (sets `createdAt = Date.now()`, `baselineWordCount = currentWordCount`, `pausedAt = null`, `pausedDurationMs = 0`), calls `saveMilestonePlan`, updates local state. 5. `editPlan`: merges partial updates into existing plan, resets `createdAt` and `baselineWordCount` to current values only if `targetWordCount` or `targetDate` changed. Rationale: if the user just tweaks the weekly cadence, preserve historical progress data. 6. `pausePlan`: sets `plan.paused = true`, `plan.pausedAt = Date.now()`, persists. 7. `resumePlan`: sets `plan.paused = false`, adds `Date.now() - pausedAt` to `pausedDurationMs`, sets `pausedAt = null`, persists. 8. `disablePlan`: calls `clearMilestonePlan(storyId)`, resets local plan to `null`. `nextChapterName` derivation (inside the memo): find the first `ChapterProgress` entry where `plannedCount + draftingCount > 0`; return its `chapter` name or `null` if all chapters are done. --- ### Step 4 — Build `MilestonePlannerSetup.tsx` Props: ```ts interface MilestonePlannerSetupProps { initialValues?: Partial>; onSave: (values: { targetWordCount: number; targetDate: number; weeklyTarget: number }) => void; onCancel?: () => void; // only present when editing existing plan isEditing: boolean; } ``` Layout (inline, no modal): - Heading: "Set up draft plan" (setup) or "Edit draft plan" (edit) - Three fields in a compact row on desktop, stacked on mobile: - "Target length" — `` — label "Words" - "Finish by" — `` — sets `targetDate` as `new Date(value).getTime()` - "Weekly writing goal" — `` — label "Words/week" - CTA: `

title: Create lightweight timeline view for chronological consistency
Add worldbuilding panel with linked story elements
Add POV and character-presence balance dashboard for draft diagnostics
description:
## Problem
Writers often discover late that POV distribution and character presence are unintentionally imbalanced, causing pacing and perspective issues that require costly rewrite passes. Current scene metadata helps capture context but does not provide manuscript-level balance diagnostics.

## Target user
Multi-POV and ensemble-cast fiction writers who need quick visibility into perspective and presence distribution during drafting/revision.

## Outcome / KPI
- Reduce late-stage rewrites caused by accidental POV/character imbalance
- Increase usage of scene metadata during active drafting
- Increase completion rate of planned revision passes

## Scope
- Add a diagnostics dashboard summarizing POV distribution across chapters/scenes
- Add character-presence heatmap by chapter with filter by major/minor characters
- Flag configurable imbalance thresholds (e.g., POV dominance, long character absence)
- Provide deep links from each diagnostic signal to the relevant scenes
- Allow writers to dismiss or snooze specific warnings per manuscript

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting scenes to fix imbalance
- Prescriptive “correct” narrative formulas
- Sentiment/style scoring in this ticket

## Acceptance criteria
- Dashboard renders POV share and character-presence views for a manuscript with scene metadata
- Writer can click any flagged item and navigate to impacted scenes
- Threshold warnings are deterministic and documented in-product
- Writer can dismiss/snooze warnings and settings persist per story
- Dashboard remains usable on manuscripts with at least 100 scenes

## Priority rationale
This is a high-value revision aid that extends existing metadata work into actionable insight, helping writers produce stronger drafts faster and differentiating beyond basic structure tools.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Create shareable read-only chapter preview links for private feedback
description:
## Problem
Writers need lightweight external feedback before publishing, but the app currently lacks a safe, low-friction way to share draft chapters without granting full edit access.

## Target user
Solo writers seeking feedback from trusted readers/critique partners during drafting and revision.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of stories that receive at least one external feedback cycle
- Reduce time from draft completion to first feedback received
- Improve retention of writers in revision stages

## Scope
- Add per-chapter `Share preview` action that creates a read-only link
- Allow writer to set optional expiration date and regenerate/revoke links
- Preview page shows chapter content only (no editor controls, no internal workspace)
- Capture lightweight reader reactions (thumbs up/down + short comment)
- Surface feedback entries back in writer’s chapter sidebar

## Non-goals
- Full public publishing/discovery feed
- Real-time collaborative editing permissions
- Advanced moderation/community management systems

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can generate, copy, and revoke a preview link per chapter
- Link opens a read-only page accessible without app account
- Expired/revoked links no longer expose chapter content
- Reader feedback submitted through preview appears in writer view within the chapter context
- Sharing controls are clearly scoped to chapter-level content only

## Priority rationale
This introduces an Inkitt-inspired feedback loop in a controlled, private form that fits MVP progression and helps writers iterate faster without expanding into full community infrastructure.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Ship manuscript export to DOCX and PDF with chapter-aware formatting
description:
## Problem
Writers need to share or submit manuscripts outside the app, but there is no dependable export path that preserves chapter structure and readable formatting. This creates lock-in anxiety and blocks practical adoption.

## Target user
Drafting and revising writers who need to send manuscripts to beta readers, editors, or submission channels in standard document formats.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of active stories that reach an export event
- Reduce churn risk signals related to data portability concerns
- Improve user satisfaction for end-to-end drafting workflow

## Scope
- Add `Export Manuscript` action at story level
- Support exports to `.docx` and `.pdf`
- Export includes title page (optional), chapter headings, scene breaks, and body text in reading order
- Provide basic formatting presets: manuscript standard and compact reading copy
- Include export confirmation with file type and timestamp

## Non-goals
- Query-package generation (synopsis, pitch letters, submission bundles)
- Advanced style customization controls
- Collaborative annotation inside exported documents

## Acceptance criteria
- User can export any story to DOCX and PDF without app crash or data loss
- Chapter/scene ordering in export matches in-app structure
- Formatting preset choice is applied correctly in output documents
- Exported files are downloadable locally with deterministic filenames
- Failed exports surface actionable error messaging and allow retry

## Priority rationale
Data portability is table-stakes for serious writers and directly supports trust in a writer-first product. A scoped, reliable export feature closes a core workflow gap without overreaching into publishing-suite complexity.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Reframe as story-aware focus sessions tied to scene objectives
description:
## Problem
Generic writing timers are now category-standard and do not reliably improve story completion quality on their own. Writers need focused sessions that connect directly to scene-level narrative outcomes, not just elapsed time and word counts.

## Target user
Fiction writers who draft in scenes and want structured sessions that help them finish specific narrative objectives (new beat, revision pass, dialogue polish) with less context-switching.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase % of sessions with completed scene objective
- Increase multi-session continuity rate (users resume the suggested next scene within 24h)
- Improve week-2 retention among users who run story-aware focus sessions

## Scope
- Add `Story-aware Focus Session` mode in the editor with optional timer presets (15/25/45 minutes)
- Require selecting a target scene and objective type before starting (e.g., draft beat, revise pacing, tighten dialogue)
- Show live progress with both words-added and objective status (in progress / complete)
- End session with recap: words added, objective completion, and suggested next-scene handoff note
- Persist recent session history per story, including objective metadata

## Non-goals
- Social leaderboards, streak competitions, or multiplayer battles
- Advanced coaching/critique generated by AI
- Cross-story analytics dashboards in v1

## Acceptance criteria
- User can start/pause/end a focus session tied to a selected scene without data loss
- Objective selection is required at session start and stored in session history
- Session recap includes objective completion status and a next-step handoff note
- Save-state visibility remains present during active session mode
- Session settings/history persist across reload for the same user and story

## Priority rationale
Competitors already offer timer-only sprint tools; reframing around scene-objective completion creates a clearer story-first advantage and aligns with our goal to ship a writer workflow optimized for drafting outcomes.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research
